option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Knovis — Project Summary
> **Purpose of this file:** single source of truth for new sessions. Read this + `SESSION_LOG.md` + `TASKS.md` first; do NOT scan the repo unless something here is insufficient or contradicted. Keep this file updated after every meaningful change.
## What it is "Your second brain" — a learning app. Log what you learn, see it as a living 3D knowledge graph, and get AI-scheduled quizzes before you forget. Gamified (daily streak). Runs in **demo mode** without sign-in (DEMO badge, seeded topics).
## Stack (fixed — do not propose alternatives) - **Next.js 16.2.10** (App Router, Turbopack) + React 19.2.4 + TypeScript 5 - **Tailwind v4** (in `dependencies`, not dev — needed for prod builds) - **Supabase** — auth (email; Google OAuth was removed) + Postgres DB (`@supabase/ssr`, `@supabase/supabase-js`) + **pgvector** for RAG. **Decision (2026-07-15): stay on Supabase, do NOT migrate to TiDB** — auth + RLS + browser-direct queries are load-bearing and TiDB has neither; when 500MB is hit, upgrade to Pro ($25/mo, 8GB, no 7-day pause) rather than migrate. - **Gemini** via `@google/genai` — quiz generation / ingest / planning + **embeddings** (`gemini-embedding-001`, 768-dim) for RAG. **Model fallback chain** in `gemini.ts`: on 429/quota or 503/overload the call retries down `GEMINI_MODEL` → `GEMINI_MODEL_FALLBACKS` (default flash → flash-lite → 2.0-flash → 2.0-flash-lite); a 429'd model is skipped for 5 min per warm instance. - **three.js 0.185** — the 3D brain scene - **Deploy target: Vercel** (`render.yaml` was removed); Supabase free tier for db+auth
## Design language Dark, organic, atmospheric — NOT boxy/generic. Mesh glows, blobs, pill shapes, organic radii. Fonts: Inter (body), **Space Grotesk** (display + canvas labels), JetBrains Mono (tiny caps labels), **Fraunces** (`--font-serif`, long-form reading on topic blog pages — see `.article-lead`/`.article-body` in globals.css). Ambient blob divs in `layout.tsx`. Background `#0b0a0e`.
**Branding (renamed from Engramia 2026-07-15):** the app is **Knovis** everywhere — knowledge + vision, deliberately echoing "novice" (the About page's origin story leans into this: every real learner stays a novice). The meaning tagline "kno·vis — knowledge meets vision" sits under the wordmark in `Nav.tsx`, on the login page, in layout metadata, and in the footer. Guest cookie is `knovis_guest`; localStorage/sessionStorage keys use a `knovis.*` prefix (tour state, demo state, notes) — renaming these was a deliberate part of the rebrand, so pre-existing guest sessions/localStorage from the Engramia era reset silently (no migration, no errors).
## Route map | Route | File | What | |---|---|---| | `/` | `src/app/page.tsx` | Today dashboard: revision plan (rows are links → `/recall?topic=`), ProgressMap, streak. Stats: Topics / Tasks today | | `/brain` | `src/app/brain/page.tsx` | 3D brain graph + search + topic info card (right panel; summarised text + "Read the full topic" → blog) | | `/blogs/[id]` | `src/app/blogs/[id]/page.tsx` | Topic "blog" — full read, Fraunces serif w/ drop cap, key ideas, connections. Breadcrumb → /blogs. CTAs: Revise / All blogs / Brain graph. | | `/blogs` | `src/app/blogs/page.tsx` | **Library page** — all topic blogs as filterable cards (search, category chips, review count). Links to `/blogs/[id]`. "Read as one story" module links to the static series (DSA, SQL, SARFAESI, Macroeconomics; **Competition Act paused from discovery 2026-07-20** — commented out of `STORY_SERIES`, route/data untouched). | | `/blogs/dsa` | `src/app/blogs/dsa/page.tsx` | **The Pattern Atlas** hub — static DSA blog series covering the NeetCode 150 roadmap (18 chapters listed in reading order, difficulty mix chips). | | `/blogs/dsa/[topic]` | `src/app/blogs/dsa/[topic]/page.tsx` | Chapter (umbrella) blog — pattern essay + prereq/unlock chips + its questions in order. SSG. | | `/blogs/dsa/[topic]/[slug]` | `src/app/blogs/dsa/[topic]/[slug]/page.tsx` | Question blog (150 of them) — full read + linear prev/next crossing chapter boundaries ("read N of 150"). SSG. | | `/notes` | `src/app/notes/page.tsx` | Personal notes — OneNote-style tree of notes + subnotes, markdown Write/Read. **localStorage only, NOT in the graph.** `?note=<id>` opens one. | | `/add` | `src/app/add/page.tsx` | Add/ingest new learning. **Guests are gated, not simulated:** submitting in demo mode shows a translucent "This is a demo" popup (Sign in / Not now) instead of a fake success — guests can look around but can't write data. | | `/recall` | `src/app/recall/page.tsx` | Quiz session (renamed from "Review" 2026-07-20; `/review` permanently redirects here): all plan items loaded (done ones show prompt, user answer, and stored answer; auto-skip); ONE batch AI grade at the end → report card listing EVERY question (full Q&A). Question kinds: open / quickfire / mcq / **truefalse** / **multi** (flashcards fully removed). | | `/about` | `src/app/about/page.tsx` | Quirky About page (Sanyam Gupta, origin story, contact CTAs: email + LinkedIn). New section (2026-07-18): a witty explainer of the SM-2 algorithm + a hand-built inline SVG chart of the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve (color-coded review curves flattening with each review vs. a "no review" baseline). Linked from the global footer. | | `/profile` | `src/app/profile/page.tsx` | View/edit display name, streak/topic stats, sign out | | `/login` | `src/app/login/page.tsx` | Email auth (no OAuth); signup captures name → `raw_user_meta_data.name` → `handle_new_user` trigger → `profiles.display_name`. "Continue as guest" sets `knovis_guest=1` cookie + **arms the guided tour (mode "demo")** + hard-navigates → demo data on production. On a browser's FIRST visit the page auto-arms the tour (mode "first") + guest cookie and redirects to `/` (branded splash while redirecting). | | `/auth/callback` | `src/app/auth/callback/route.ts` | Supabase callback | | API | `src/app/api/{ingest,plan,quiz}/route.ts` | See "AI-call budget" below — only ingest & quiz-finish call Gemini | | API | `src/app/api/rag/reindex/route.ts` | Self-serve zero-downtime RAG reindex (auth-gated, own rows only) — see RAG v2 below. Zero AI calls (embeddings only). |
## AI-call budget (deliberate design — keep it this way) Exactly **1 Gemini call per ingest** and **1 per finished quiz session**; everything else is zero-AI: - **Ingest** (`/api/ingest`): the single `extractKnowledge()` call returns a **question bank** (7-8 per topic: ~2 open, ~1 quickfire, ~2 MCQ (4 options + correct_index), ~1-2 **truefalse** (options ["True","False"] + correct_index), ~1 **multi** (4-5 options + correct_indices array); each with model_answer + difficulty) and **facts** (1-2/topic) → stored in `questions` & `facts` tables. Flashcards are GONE (no extraction, no table writes). - **Quiz** (`/api/quiz` actions): `start` picks bank questions (least-asked, review-count-matched difficulty, kind rotation open→mcq→truefalse→quickfire→multi; synthesized local fallback for pre-bank topics) into a `quiz_sessions` snapshot (client never sees answers); `answer` upserts each answer into `quiz_answers` as submitted (multi picks are JSON-serialised into the `answer` text column — no schema change); `finish` grades choice kinds (mcq/truefalse exact, multi with partial credit: exact=5, else 0-4 by hits−wrong over key size) deterministically + ONE `gradeSession()` batch call for typed answers, gated at `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT`=1/day (keyword-overlap fallback if Gemini fails OR the daily cap is already spent, call skipped entirely if nothing typed) → report card (score_pct, summary, strengths/focus, per-item full Q&A) saved on the session (idempotent, `ai_graded` flag set iff `gradeSession()` actually ran), SRS per topic from avg score, reviews rows, bank usage bumped. The old `rate` (flashcard) action is removed. - **Plan** (`/api/plan`): Gemini narrative REMOVED — headline from day-rotating templates, insight reuses a `topic_links.reason` between today's topics (`composeNarrative()`). - **Fact of the day**: `getFactOfTheDay()` in data.ts picks deterministically by day from `facts` (joined topic name; fallback = a topic key_point). Dashboard card, right column. Zero AI.
## RAG layer (added 2026-07-15, hardened 2026-07-15 — `supabase/schema-rag.sql` THEN `schema-rag-v2.sql` must both be run to activate) User content is the source of truth. Everything ingested (article/notes) and every typed answer is chunked, embedded and stored so generation RETRIEVES the user's own material instead of leaning on the model's world knowledge. **Strictly additive + best-effort:** if either schema file isn't run yet, or embedding fails, every path degrades to the old behaviour (no errors). - `src/lib/chunk.ts` — dependency-free overlapping chunker (~1400 chars ≈ 350 tokens, 200-char overlap, paragraph/sentence aware). - `src/lib/gemini.ts` `embedTexts()/embedText()` — Gemini embeddings, L2-normalised client-side (truncated 768-dim isn't auto-normalised), `RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT` vs `RETRIEVAL_QUERY` task types. - `src/lib/rag.ts` — chunk/embed/store (`indexContent()`, `indexAnswers()`), retrieval (`retrieveContext()`, `formatContext()`), plus the v2 additions below. All catch-and-continue; recognise "RAG not installed" (42P01/42883) and stay quiet. - **Wiring:** `/api/ingest` retrieves the user's prior related chunks and passes them to `extractKnowledge(text, existingTopics, priorContext)` as grounding (skipped on first ingest — this retrieval stays on the critical path, it shapes the prompt), then indexes the new entry. **Indexing itself runs in Next's `after()`, deferred until after the response is sent** (fixed 2026-07-15 — it was blocking the response and contributing to a 504 `FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT` on link ingests). `/api/quiz` finish indexes typed (open/quickfire) answers (still synchronous — quiz finish isn't the tight path link ingest is). - Blog long-form generation from RAG is deliberately NOT wired (would add an AI call per page view, breaking the zero-AI-render budget) — see TASKS backlog.
### RAG v2 — document registry, index versioning, hybrid search, observability (added 2026-07-15, `supabase/schema-rag-v2.sql`) Hardens the RAG layer against three gaps found on review: no dedup, vector-only retrieval, and zero observability. **Cross-encoder reranking was evaluated and deliberately deferred** (no ML runtime in this stack; a Gemini-based reranker would add a 2nd AI call per ingest, breaking the "exactly 1 Gemini call per ingest" budget) — see TASKS backlog. - **Document registry + content-hash dedup:** `rag_documents` table (one row per indexed entry/answer, `content_hash` = sha256 via `node:crypto`). `findDuplicateEntry()` in `rag.ts` is checked in `/api/ingest` BEFORE the Gemini call — resubmitting identical text/URL now reuses the existing entry (200 + `duplicate:true` + friendly message, `xp:0`) instead of creating a duplicate `entries`/`knowledge_chunks`/`questions` row every time (a real bug that existed until now). `upsertDocument()` (private) makes `indexContent()` idempotent: re-indexing the same `entryId` deletes-then-replaces its chunks rather than duplicating. Partial unique index scopes the hash constraint to `source='entry' and status='active'` so answer chunks (which can legitimately repeat) aren't affected. - **Alias-based index versioning:** `knowledge_chunks.index_version` + per-user `rag_index_pointers(user_id, active_version)` — the "alias" a reindex flips atomically. New ingests always tag chunks with the CURRENT `getActiveIndexVersion()`. `POST /api/rag/reindex` (new route, self-serve/RLS-only, no service-role key) builds a new generation in the background (`reindexDocument()`, paginated 200 docs/call, safe to call repeatedly) and only swaps the pointer + prunes the retired version once a full pass leaves zero pending documents — old version keeps serving every live retrieval throughout. Infrastructure for a future need (chunk-size/embedding-dim changes); nothing triggers it automatically today. - **Hybrid search:** `match_knowledge_chunks_hybrid` SQL function — Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) between vector kNN and Postgres `tsvector`/`ts_rank_cd` keyword search (the standard BM25 stand-in; true BM25 isn't native to Postgres). Query lexemes are OR'd (not AND'd like `plainto_tsquery`/`websearch_to_tsquery`) since the real caller passes a ~2000-char grounding excerpt, not a short phrase. `retrieveContext()` tries hybrid first, falls back to the (now version-aware) plain-vector `match_knowledge_chunks` via the existing `isMissingRag()` catch if the new SQL hasn't been applied yet. - **Observability:** `rag_retrieval_logs` (chunk-level attribution: query, mode, index_version, per-chunk id/similarity/rank as jsonb — written by `retrieveContext()`, `entry_id` backfilled via `linkRetrievalLogToEntry()` once the ingest's entry exists, since retrieval runs before the entry does). `getRetrievalMetrics()` aggregates recent logs in JS (avg similarity, zero-hit rate, by-mode/by-day breakdown). `rag_index_version_correlation()` SQL function + `getIndexVersionCorrelation()` wrapper join retrieval logs → grounded topics → `reviews.score` by `index_version` — **explicitly correlation, not causal regression detection** (several steps removed: retrieval quality → extraction → question quality → the user's own recall). **No new UI** — these are queryable diagnostics only, matching how this app has no admin dashboard anywhere. - `knowledge_chunks` table + HNSW cosine index + both match functions + `rag_documents`/`rag_index_pointers`/`rag_retrieval_logs`, all RLS-scoped to `auth.uid()`.
## Key modules - `src/components/BrainScene.tsx` (~850 lines) — the crown jewel. Stylised brain as particle shell; topics = glowing cortex patches; selection dives camera inside; label system: canvas-texture sprites in Space Grotesk, per-frame **screen-space declutter** (priority place → vertical nudge via `sprite.center` → fade), staggered edge labels, **labels are click targets** (raycast before glows, only when opacity > 0.3). Yaw bias +0.32 on landscape keeps selection out from under the info card. - `src/components/ProgressMap.tsx` — streak Momentum card + **streak-milestone progress bar** (ladder 3/7/14/21/30/50/75/100/150/200/365; "N days to a M-day streak" + record-based motivational line) + 16-week review activity heatmap. - `src/components/ProgressCalendar.tsx` — month-view study calendar (days lit by review count, month nav, today ring). **Lit days are buttons** → modal with that day's full report card via `getDayReport()`. Rendered only after profile loads to dodge SSR date mismatch. - `src/components/ReportCardView.tsx` — shared report-card renderer (score ring header + per-question cards incl. truefalse/multi option highlighting, partial-credit chip). Used by /recall report phase AND the calendar day modal. Exports `KIND_LABEL`. - `src/components/Footer.tsx` — global footer in `layout.tsx` (brand + kno·vis meaning, Explore links, Connect: /about, mailto contact, LinkedIn). - `getDayReport(day)` in data.ts — merges ALL graded `quiz_sessions` reports of a day (fallback: rebuild from `reviews` rows); `getLatestReportToday()` delegates to it, so "See report" shows every question asked today. `getTopicQuestions(topicId)` returns the bank WITH answers for the blog Q&A section (`select *` so a missing correct_indices column degrades gracefully). - `src/components/Nav.tsx` — top nav (Today/Brain/Add/Review + Profile/Sign-in pill; mobile 5th tab). Includes `authStatus` logic to correctly display 'Sign in' when unauthenticated in production. - `src/lib/text.ts` — `stripMarkdown()`; Gemini sometimes emits markdown and daily plans are cached in `daily_plans`, so it's applied both in `/api/plan` before saving AND at render time on the dashboard. - `src/components/RichText.tsx` — inline-markdown renderer (**bold**/*italic*/`code` → styled spans). Used for the dashboard "The thread today" insight instead of stripping (preserves emphasis). Recursive first-match parser; strips leading `#`/bullets. - `src/app/recall/page.tsx` (renamed from `review/` 2026-07-20) — reads `?topic=<id>` from `window.location.search`; single-task mode when matched (`singleTask` flag suppresses `markPlanCompleted`). Full-plan mode now includes ALL plan items (not just remaining): done items show an "Already reviewed today" phase and auto-advance; quiz session started only for pending (non-done) topic IDs. - `src/app/blogs/page.tsx` — standalone topic-blog library page. Filterable by search and category chip. Cards show mastery bar + review count. Links to `/blogs/[id]`. Added as **Blogs** (BookIcon) in `Nav.tsx` LINKS between Brain and Add. - `src/lib/dsa/` — **The Pattern Atlas content module** (added 2026-07-19): `types.ts` (DsaTopic/DsaProblem, `DSA_DIFFICULTY_COLORS`), `topics/*.ts` (18 files, one per NeetCode-150 chapter — all 150 question blogs as markdown-in-TS data, verified against live neetcode.io incl. current grouping: Stack=6, Backtracking=10), `index.ts` (ordered `DSA_TOPICS` registry + linear-nav helpers `dsaReadingOrder`/`dsaNeighbors`/`dsaProblemNumber`/`dsaStopHref`). Pure static data → SSG pages, zero AI/DB. Bodies render via `Markdown.tsx` inside `.article-body`. To add/edit content: edit the topic file; routes pick it up automatically. **Full hellointerview-style rewrite complete (2026-07-21):** every one of the 150 question bodies follows a `**Signal.**` / `**Brute force.**` / `**Optimal approach.**` / `viz:*` diagram / `**Complexity.**` / `**Thread.**` structure (was patchy prose with only 44/150 diagrams before); the four chapters still on an older ASCII-art/Python-fence template (`two-pointers.ts`, `binary-search.ts`, `dp-1d.ts`, `dp-2d.ts`) were normalized to house style in the same pass. Some chapter files also carry a hardcoded `questions: [...]` quiz array per problem (added by a separate process, unrelated to the story content — leave it alone when editing bodies). - `src/lib/notes.ts` — **personal notes, localStorage-backed** (`knovis.notes.v1`), deliberately outside the graph/Supabase. CRUD + `childrenOf`/`countDescendants` tree helpers; `ensureSeeded()` writes a one-time welcome note; every write dispatches `NOTES_EVENT` (+ native `storage` event) so the /notes page and the dashboard "Personal notes" section stay in sync. `Note` type in `types.ts` (`parent_id` = subnote nesting). - `src/components/Markdown.tsx` — dependency-free block+inline markdown renderer (headings, lists, blockquote, fenced code, hr, bold/italic/code/links). Used by the notes Read view. (`RichText.tsx` is the smaller inline-only variant for the dashboard insight.) **`viz:*` diagram fences** (added 2026-07-20): a fence tagged `viz:<kind>` (e.g. ` ```viz:array `) dispatches its JSON body to `src/components/viz/VizBlock.tsx` instead of rendering as code — `ArrayViz` (stepper), `TreeViz`, `TableDiffViz`, `FlowViz` primitives, shape-validated by `viz/types.ts`. `Markdown` takes `strictViz`/`vizAccent` props: the 10 hand-authored story-content call sites (DSA/SQL/Macro/SARFAESI/Competition-Act) pass `strictViz` so a malformed payload throws and fails the SSG build; the notes page (user-generated content) doesn't, so a bad fence there renders an inline error card instead of crashing. **Full rollout complete (2026-07-20):** all 34 DSA/SQL/Macro chapter files now have diagrams (91 total: 44 DSA, 41 SQL, 6 Macro) plus an end-to-end accuracy pass (8 real content errors found and fixed) — see SESSION_LOG for the per-file breakdown. `scripts/validate-viz.mts` fast-validates every `viz:*` payload in a set of files without a full Next build (reuses the real `parseVizPayload()` validator). - `getTopicSource(topicId)` in `data.ts` → `TopicSource` (`{kind:"url",url}` | `{kind:"text"}` | null): the blog page shows where a topic came from. Real mode joins `entry_topics`→`entries.source_url`; demo uses `demoTopicSource` (a few arxiv/github URLs, rest text). - `src/lib/srs.ts` — SM-2 spaced repetition (mastery calculations disabled). `src/lib/progress.ts` — `advanceStreak()` (renamed from `awardXp` 2026-07-16 when XP was removed entirely — streaks and active days, no XP field anywhere). - **RAG tables** — Run `supabase/schema-rag.sql` (creates `vector` extension, `knowledge_chunks` table + HNSW index + `match_knowledge_chunks` function, RLS). Safe to re-run. Until it's run, RAG is dormant and the app behaves exactly as before. - **Quiz DB tables** — Run `supabase/schema-quiz-tables-only.sql` in your Supabase SQL editor (this file avoids policy conflicts if your database already has policies): creates `questions` (bank: kind/options/correct_index/**correct_indices jsonb**/model_answer/difficulty/times_asked), `facts`, `quiz_sessions` (items snapshot + report jsonb), `quiz_answers` (unique session_id+question_index), with RLS policies and indexes. Both schema files include `alter table questions add column if not exists correct_indices jsonb` for older DBs. Alternatively, run the full `supabase/schema.sql` if starting fresh. - Quiz types in `types.ts`: `QuestionKind` (open|quickfire|mcq|truefalse|multi), `SessionQuestion` (client-safe, no answers), `QuizSession`, `ReportItem` (+selected_indices/correct_indices), `ReportCard`, `TopicQuestion` (bank WITH answers, for blogs), `DailyFact`; `Flashcard` type removed; `ExtractionResult` has `questions` + `facts` (no flashcards). - **Guided tour (added 2026-07-15, interactivity + review step 2026-07-15):** `src/lib/tour.ts` (14-step script incl. cross-page steps + a fully self-contained `custom: "review-demo"` step with its own made-up question `DEMO_REVIEW_QUESTION` + storage helpers: seen flag in localStorage `knovis.tour.seen.v1`, active state in sessionStorage `knovis.tour.state.v1` so it survives the guest-mode hard navigations) + `src/components/TutorialTour.tsx` (mounted once in `layout.tsx`; dim overlay + glowing spotlight cut-out via giant box-shadow, translucent blurred `.tour-card` in globals.css, progress dots, Back/Next/skip, Esc/arrow keys; polls `[data-tour="…"]` anchors every 150 ms so late-loading client data & route changes are handled, picks the visible anchor when desktop/mobile share one, centered fallback if an anchor never appears). Anchors: `nav` (Nav desktop+mobile), `plan`/`recall-cta`/`momentum`/`calendar`/`fact`/`notes` (dashboard), `brain-canvas`/`brain-search` (/brain), `blogs-intro` (/blogs), `add-form` (/add). **Flows:** first browser visit → login page arms mode "first" + guest cookie → tour runs over demo pages → complete/skip clears guest cookie → `/login`; every "Continue as guest" click arms mode "demo" → tour replays → finish stays in demo. TutorialTour also self-arms once for unseen visitors already in demo mode (abandoned mid-tour in another tab). - **Spotlighted features stay genuinely interactive** (calendar day → real report-card modal, brain canvas/search, add-form inputs): the overlay wrapper is `pointer-events:none` whenever a spotlight target exists (only `.tour-card` re-enables `pointer-events:auto` on itself via CSS), so clicks fall straight through to the real page. `ProgressCalendar`'s day-report modal was bumped to `z-[125]` (was `z-50`) so it renders above the tour's `z-120` dim layer instead of being hidden by it. - **Link navigation is blocked globally while the tour is active** (capture-phase `document` click listener in TutorialTour checks `target.closest("a")` and calls `preventDefault`/`stopPropagation`) so a stray click on the Nav, a plan row, a blog card, or "Read the full topic" can't route the visitor away mid-tour — non-anchor interactions (buttons, canvas, inputs) are untouched. Opt out per-element with a `data-allow-nav` ancestor (used by the /add demo-gate popup's "Sign in" link). - **"Try a review" step** (`custom: "review-demo"`, inserted right after "One-tap review"): a self-contained mock quiz rendered directly inside `.tour-card` (`ReviewDemoCard` in TutorialTour.tsx) — never touches real quiz state/Supabase. Pick an option → Submit → mocked report card (score ring via `BigScoreRing`, feedback, correct answer) → Continue. - `src/lib/demo.ts` — demo-mode seed data. `src/lib/data.ts` — data access. - `src/lib/types.ts` — `Topic`, `TopicLink`, `categoryColor(category)`. - `src/lib/supabase/{client,server}.ts`, `src/lib/gemini.ts`. - `src/proxy.ts` — Next 16 proxy (migrated from middleware.ts). Auth gate honours the `knovis_guest` cookie (guest/demo access without an account) and deletes it as soon as a real session exists. - **Guest mode / isDemo:** `isDemo` (demo.ts) = no `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` OR `knovis_guest=1` cookie. Cookie helpers `enableGuestMode()`/`clearGuestMode()`. Guest→signed-in transitions use full page loads so the module-level constant re-evaluates. Render-time isDemo checks (Nav, /add, /profile) go through post-mount state to keep hydration stable. - **Plan done-tracking:** `PlanItem.done` computed at read time (today's `reviews` rows in real mode; `demoState.done` in demo). Dashboard plan section: green dots = remaining, done rows dimmed/✓/last, 5 rows + "Show all" toggle (plan caps at 10 items). Full /recall runs cover only remaining items.
## Security posture (reviewed 2026-07-15, hardened 2026-07-18) - **SSRF guard on link ingest, DNS-rebinding-safe (`src/app/api/ingest/route.ts`, fixed 2026-07-18):** `assertPublicHttpUrl()` is only the cheap string-level pre-filter (protocol, credentials, port, literal private IPs, `.local`/`.internal`). The REAL boundary is `ssrfSafeDispatcher` — a custom `undici` `Agent` whose `connect.lookup` resolves DNS itself, rejects the request if ANY resolved address is loopback/private/link-local/CGNAT, and only then hands the validated IP to the actual TCP connect (same lookup call does both, so there's no second, unvalidated DNS query for an attacker's server to answer differently). Closes the bypass where a hostname string looks harmless but its DNS record points at `169.254.169.254`/`127.0.0.1`/internal ranges — the old hostname-only check couldn't catch that. TLS SNI/cert hostname validation is unaffected (Node derives those from the URL, not from what `lookup` returns). Verified against real DNS-rebinding test domains (`*.nip.io`, which resolves to whatever IP is embedded in the subdomain) — metadata/loopback/private targets all blocked, real public HTTPS sites (incl. TLS) still work. - **Per-user daily Gemini-call caps, split 9 + 1 (`INGEST_DAILY_LIMIT`=9, `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT`=1, added 2026-07-18):** ingest capped at 9/day (down from an unbounded-in-practice 20); quiz AI grading (`gradeSession()`) separately capped at 1/day via a new `quiz_sessions.ai_graded` flag — deliberately NOT shared with the ingest budget, so a heavy ingest day can never fully starve the review flow. Exceeding the quiz cap never blocks finishing a session — it silently reuses the existing heuristic (keyword-overlap) grader, the same fallback already used when Gemini itself errors. Both day-boundaries now use the CLIENT's local timezone (`clampTz`/`localTodayForOffset`/`dayStartUtcIso` from `dates.ts`) — the ingest cap previously reset at the server's own midnight (effectively UTC on Vercel), not the user's. Hitting the ingest cap returns `resetAt` (ISO instant of the user's next local midnight) in the 429 body; `/add` shows a popup ("You can add more starting tomorrow at —") instead of just inline error text, via a new `ApiError` class in `data.ts` that carries the HTTP status so the UI can branch on 429 without parsing message text. - **`PUBLIC_PATHS` exact/segment match, not prefix match** (`src/proxy.ts`, fixed 2026-07-18): was `path.startsWith(p)`, which would treat a hypothetical future route like `/login-help` as public just for sharing a text prefix with `/login`. Now `path === p || path.startsWith(p + "/")`. - All API routes wrap `request.json()` in try/catch (malformed-body → 400). `/auth/callback` redirects to `/login?error=auth` when `exchangeCodeForSession` fails instead of silently landing on the dashboard. - RLS: every table `own <x>` policy is `auth.uid() = user_id` (profiles: `= id`; entry_topics via entry ownership). data.ts queries Supabase directly from the browser — RLS is the only thing isolating users, so keep it on every new table (the new `knowledge_chunks` has it). - **Ingest guardrails (added 2026-07-16, fixed 2026-07-16, `src/lib/guardrails.ts`):** `looksLikeGibberish()` — dependency-free heuristics (repeated-pattern runs, meaningful-char ratio, distinct-word ratio) reject obvious spam/keyboard-mashing before it burns a Gemini call or pollutes the knowledge base; deliberately lenient (dense technical text, code, LaTeX, non-English prose all pass — tested against a case suite). **Two false-positive bugs fixed same day** (found via a real link-ingest of an external blog post that got wrongly rejected): (1) the repeated-pattern-flood regex ran on ANY 10+ char repeat including pure punctuation, so ASCII fraction bars/table borders/box-drawing dividers common in technical writing tripped it — now requires the repeated unit to contain a letter/digit; (2) the distinct-character-ratio check (since removed entirely) was not scale-invariant — English has only 36 possible alnum characters, so ANY real prose past ~2 sentences (~300 chars) mathematically saturates the alphabet and its ratio collapses below the threshold regardless of legitimacy; its intended purpose was already redundantly covered by the flood-regex and the distinct-word-ratio check, so it was dropped rather than re-tuned. `sanitizeField()` strips control chars and clamps length, applied to EVERY field `extractKnowledge()` returns (title, summary, topic name/summary/key_points, connection reason, question prompt/model_answer/options, fact text) before DB insert; topic `category` is validated against `CATEGORY_COLORS` keys (`src/lib/types.ts`) with a `"General"` fallback — previously unvalidated. **Prompt-injection hardening** in `extractKnowledge()` (`gemini.ts`): the pasted/fetched text and the RAG grounding block are explicitly framed as "untrusted data, not instructions" with a rule telling the model to never follow embedded commands (relevant since link ingestion feeds arbitrary third-party webpage text into the prompt). Blast radius if a prompt injection ever succeeded is contained — no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` anywhere in the app (verified by grep), so AI-generated text can only ever render as inert text via React's default escaping, with one exception below. - **Markdown link scheme validation** (`src/components/Markdown.tsx`, used by `/notes`): `isSafeHref()` only renders `[text](url)` as a clickable `<a>` when the scheme is `http`/`https`/`mailto` (or scheme-less/relative) — a `javascript:`/`data:` URI now renders as inert plain text instead of an executable link. Verified live: a `javascript:` and a `data:text/html,<script>` link both render as non-clickable text while a normal `https://` link still works.
## Dates / "today" (fixed 2026-07-15) - `src/lib/dates.ts` is the single source for day boundaries. The app's "day" is the USER'S LOCAL calendar day, never the server's UTC day. Client passes its tz offset (`new Date().getTimezoneOffset()`) to `/api/plan?tz=` and quiz finish/complete bodies; server uses `localTodayForOffset`/`dayStartUtcIso`/`clampTz`. Client bucketing (calendar, heatmap, review detail) uses `localDayKey(new Date(created_at))`. This was the root cause of "answered questions show as not attempted": a UTC-vs-local day mismatch (e.g. evening IST reviews landing on the next UTC date) plus done-tracking that only read `reviews`. `doneTopicsToday()` now unions `reviews` rows AND `topics.last_reviewed_at` (always written on finish) so a failed reviews insert can't strand items.
## Conventions / gotchas - Dev server: use `.claude/launch.json` name `synapse-dev` (autoPort; 3000 often taken by other projects). - **Recall page** (`src/app/recall/page.tsx`, renamed from "Review" 2026-07-20 — `/review` permanently redirects to `/recall`): reads `?topic=` via `useSearchParams` (Suspense-wrapped) so switching between `/recall?topic=A`, `?topic=B` and `/recall` re-runs the init effect while the page stays mounted (fixes the "clicking a different question reroutes to the old one" bug — the old `window.location.search` read only ran on first mount). Full-plan runs sort pending-first so the session never opens on an "already reviewed" card; a single deep-linked task that turns out to be the LAST pending topic now marks the day complete itself. - **Demo state persists** to `localStorage` (`knovis.demo.v1`) so guest progress survives reloads/hard-navigations: reviews/reports/streak carry over; `done` flags + `completed` reset on a new local day (mirrors the real backend). `saveDemoState()` after every demo mutation; `loadDemoState()` on module init when `isDemo`. - Knowledge graph of this repo lives in `graphify-out/` (`graph.json`); query it instead of grepping. **The graphify CLI is installed** (PyPI `graphifyy`, double-y) at `/Users/bucc/.local/bin/graphify` — if not on PATH in a fresh shell, call it by full path or `export PATH="/Users/bucc/.local/bin:$PATH"`. Use `graphify query "…"` / `path` / `explain`; run `graphify update .` after code changes (AST-only, no API cost). NB: an unrelated `graphify` npm package (a "Random Graph Generator") is in the global npm dir with no bin — ignore it. - Verify visual changes in the browser (demo mode works logged-out). - **Notes are localStorage-only** (per-browser, no server/graph). Cross-device sync would need a Supabase `notes` table — deliberately deferred (see TASKS backlog). - `npx tsc --noEmit` for typecheck; no test suite yet.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Knovis — Project Summary
> **Purpose of this file:** single source of truth for new sessions. Read this + `SESSION_LOG.md` + `TASKS.md` first; do NOT scan the repo unless something here is insufficient or contradicted. Keep this file updated after every meaningful change.
## What it is "Your second brain" — a learning app. Log what you learn, see it as a living 3D knowledge graph, and get AI-scheduled quizzes before you forget. Gamified (daily streak). Runs in **demo mode** without sign-in (DEMO badge, seeded topics).
## Stack (fixed — do not propose alternatives) - **Next.js 16.2.10** (App Router, Turbopack) + React 19.2.4 + TypeScript 5 - **Tailwind v4** (in `dependencies`, not dev — needed for prod builds) - **Supabase** — auth (email; Google OAuth was removed) + Postgres DB (`@supabase/ssr`, `@supabase/supabase-js`) + **pgvector** for RAG. **Decision (2026-07-15): stay on Supabase, do NOT migrate to TiDB** — auth + RLS + browser-direct queries are load-bearing and TiDB has neither; when 500MB is hit, upgrade to Pro ($25/mo, 8GB, no 7-day pause) rather than migrate. - **Gemini** via `@google/genai` — quiz generation / ingest / planning + **embeddings** (`gemini-embedding-001`, 768-dim) for RAG. **Model fallback chain** in `gemini.ts`: on 429/quota or 503/overload the call retries down `GEMINI_MODEL` → `GEMINI_MODEL_FALLBACKS` (default flash → flash-lite → 2.0-flash → 2.0-flash-lite); a 429'd model is skipped for 5 min per warm instance. - **three.js 0.185** — the 3D brain scene - **Deploy target: Vercel** (`render.yaml` was removed); Supabase free tier for db+auth
## Design language Dark, organic, atmospheric — NOT boxy/generic. Mesh glows, blobs, pill shapes, organic radii. Fonts: Inter (body), **Space Grotesk** (display + canvas labels), JetBrains Mono (tiny caps labels), **Fraunces** (`--font-serif`, long-form reading on topic blog pages — see `.article-lead`/`.article-body` in globals.css). Ambient blob divs in `layout.tsx`. Background `#0b0a0e`.
**Branding (renamed from Engramia 2026-07-15):** the app is **Knovis** everywhere — knowledge + vision, deliberately echoing "novice" (the About page's origin story leans into this: every real learner stays a novice). The meaning tagline "kno·vis — knowledge meets vision" sits under the wordmark in `Nav.tsx`, on the login page, in layout metadata, and in the footer. Guest cookie is `knovis_guest`; localStorage/sessionStorage keys use a `knovis.*` prefix (tour state, demo state, notes) — renaming these was a deliberate part of the rebrand, so pre-existing guest sessions/localStorage from the Engramia era reset silently (no migration, no errors).
## Route map | Route | File | What | |---|---|---| | `/` | `src/app/page.tsx` | Today dashboard: revision plan (rows are links → `/recall?topic=`), ProgressMap, streak. Stats: Topics / Tasks today | | `/brain` | `src/app/brain/page.tsx` | 3D brain graph + search + topic info card (right panel; summarised text + "Read the full topic" → blog) | | `/blogs/[id]` | `src/app/blogs/[id]/page.tsx` | Topic "blog" — full read, Fraunces serif w/ drop cap, key ideas, connections. Breadcrumb → /blogs. CTAs: Revise / All blogs / Brain graph. | | `/blogs` | `src/app/blogs/page.tsx` | **Library page** — all topic blogs as filterable cards (search, category chips, review count). Links to `/blogs/[id]`. "Read as one story" module links to the static series (DSA, SQL, SARFAESI, Macroeconomics; **Competition Act paused from discovery 2026-07-20** — commented out of `STORY_SERIES`, route/data untouched). | | `/blogs/dsa` | `src/app/blogs/dsa/page.tsx` | **The Pattern Atlas** hub — static DSA blog series covering the NeetCode 150 roadmap (18 chapters listed in reading order, difficulty mix chips). | | `/blogs/dsa/[topic]` | `src/app/blogs/dsa/[topic]/page.tsx` | Chapter (umbrella) blog — pattern essay + prereq/unlock chips + its questions in order. SSG. | | `/blogs/dsa/[topic]/[slug]` | `src/app/blogs/dsa/[topic]/[slug]/page.tsx` | Question blog (150 of them) — full read + linear prev/next crossing chapter boundaries ("read N of 150"). SSG. | | `/notes` | `src/app/notes/page.tsx` | Personal notes — OneNote-style tree of notes + subnotes, markdown Write/Read. **localStorage only, NOT in the graph.** `?note=<id>` opens one. | | `/add` | `src/app/add/page.tsx` | Add/ingest new learning. **Guests are gated, not simulated:** submitting in demo mode shows a translucent "This is a demo" popup (Sign in / Not now) instead of a fake success — guests can look around but can't write data. | | `/recall` | `src/app/recall/page.tsx` | Quiz session (renamed from "Review" 2026-07-20; `/review` permanently redirects here): all plan items loaded (done ones show prompt, user answer, and stored answer; auto-skip). **Per-series report cards (2026-07-21):** plan items are grouped by story series and EACH group runs its OWN independent quiz session (`GroupRunner`) — submitting a section finishes + grades + shows THAT section's report card inline immediately, independent of the other sections. Question kinds: open / quickfire / mcq / **truefalse** / **multi** (flashcards fully removed). | | `/about` | `src/app/about/page.tsx` | Quirky About page (Sanyam Gupta, origin story, contact CTAs: email + LinkedIn). New section (2026-07-18): a witty explainer of the SM-2 algorithm + a hand-built inline SVG chart of the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve (color-coded review curves flattening with each review vs. a "no review" baseline). Linked from the global footer. | | `/profile` | `src/app/profile/page.tsx` | View/edit display name, streak/topic stats, sign out | | `/login` | `src/app/login/page.tsx` | Email auth (no OAuth); signup captures name → `raw_user_meta_data.name` → `handle_new_user` trigger → `profiles.display_name`. "Continue as guest" sets `knovis_guest=1` cookie + **arms the guided tour (mode "demo")** + hard-navigates → demo data on production. On a browser's FIRST visit the page auto-arms the tour (mode "first") + guest cookie and redirects to `/` (branded splash while redirecting). | | `/auth/callback` | `src/app/auth/callback/route.ts` | Supabase callback | | API | `src/app/api/{ingest,plan,quiz}/route.ts` | See "AI-call budget" below — only ingest & quiz-finish call Gemini | | API | `src/app/api/rag/reindex/route.ts` | Self-serve zero-downtime RAG reindex (auth-gated, own rows only) — see RAG v2 below. Zero AI calls (embeddings only). |
## AI-call budget (deliberate design — keep it this way) Exactly **1 Gemini call per ingest** and **1 per finished quiz session**; everything else is zero-AI: - **Ingest** (`/api/ingest`): the single `extractKnowledge()` call returns a **question bank** (7-8 per topic: ~2 open, ~1 quickfire, ~2 MCQ (4 options + correct_index), ~1-2 **truefalse** (options ["True","False"] + correct_index), ~1 **multi** (4-5 options + correct_indices array); each with model_answer + difficulty) and **facts** (1-2/topic) → stored in `questions` & `facts` tables. Flashcards are GONE (no extraction, no table writes). - **Quiz** (`/api/quiz` actions): `start` picks bank questions (least-asked, review-count-matched difficulty, kind rotation open→mcq→truefalse→quickfire→multi; synthesized local fallback for pre-bank topics) into a `quiz_sessions` snapshot (client never sees answers); `answer` upserts each answer into `quiz_answers` as submitted (multi picks are JSON-serialised into the `answer` text column — no schema change); `finish` grades choice kinds (mcq/truefalse exact, multi with partial credit: exact=5, else 0-4 by hits−wrong over key size) deterministically + ONE `gradeSession()` batch call for typed answers, gated at `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT`=1/day (keyword-overlap fallback if Gemini fails OR the daily cap is already spent, call skipped entirely if nothing typed) → report card (score_pct, summary, strengths/focus, per-item full Q&A) saved on the session (idempotent, `ai_graded` flag set iff `gradeSession()` actually ran), SRS per topic from avg score, reviews rows, bank usage bumped. The old `rate` (flashcard) action is removed. **Since 2026-07-21 `/recall` starts one session per story-series group, so a full run finishes several sessions → up to N `finish` calls. The AI-grading budget is now per-user dynamic: `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_BASE` (env `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT`, default 1 = the General section) + one slot per row in `user_stories` (started stories) — so every section a user can submit gets a real AI-graded report; exceeding it still falls back to the heuristic grader.** - **Plan** (`/api/plan`): Gemini narrative REMOVED — headline from day-rotating templates, insight reuses a `topic_links.reason` between today's topics (`composeNarrative()`). - **Fact of the day**: `getFactOfTheDay()` in data.ts picks deterministically by day from `facts` (joined topic name; fallback = a topic key_point). Dashboard card, right column. Zero AI.
## RAG layer (added 2026-07-15, hardened 2026-07-15 — `supabase/schema-rag.sql` THEN `schema-rag-v2.sql` must both be run to activate) User content is the source of truth. Everything ingested (article/notes) and every typed answer is chunked, embedded and stored so generation RETRIEVES the user's own material instead of leaning on the model's world knowledge. **Strictly additive + best-effort:** if either schema file isn't run yet, or embedding fails, every path degrades to the old behaviour (no errors). - `src/lib/chunk.ts` — dependency-free overlapping chunker (~1400 chars ≈ 350 tokens, 200-char overlap, paragraph/sentence aware). - `src/lib/gemini.ts` `embedTexts()/embedText()` — Gemini embeddings, L2-normalised client-side (truncated 768-dim isn't auto-normalised), `RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT` vs `RETRIEVAL_QUERY` task types. - `src/lib/rag.ts` — chunk/embed/store (`indexContent()`, `indexAnswers()`), retrieval (`retrieveContext()`, `formatContext()`), plus the v2 additions below. All catch-and-continue; recognise "RAG not installed" (42P01/42883) and stay quiet. - **Wiring:** `/api/ingest` retrieves the user's prior related chunks and passes them to `extractKnowledge(text, existingTopics, priorContext)` as grounding (skipped on first ingest — this retrieval stays on the critical path, it shapes the prompt), then indexes the new entry. **Indexing itself runs in Next's `after()`, deferred until after the response is sent** (fixed 2026-07-15 — it was blocking the response and contributing to a 504 `FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT` on link ingests). `/api/quiz` finish indexes typed (open/quickfire) answers (still synchronous — quiz finish isn't the tight path link ingest is). - Blog long-form generation from RAG is deliberately NOT wired (would add an AI call per page view, breaking the zero-AI-render budget) — see TASKS backlog.
### RAG v2 — document registry, index versioning, hybrid search, observability (added 2026-07-15, `supabase/schema-rag-v2.sql`) Hardens the RAG layer against three gaps found on review: no dedup, vector-only retrieval, and zero observability. **Cross-encoder reranking was evaluated and deliberately deferred** (no ML runtime in this stack; a Gemini-based reranker would add a 2nd AI call per ingest, breaking the "exactly 1 Gemini call per ingest" budget) — see TASKS backlog. - **Document registry + content-hash dedup:** `rag_documents` table (one row per indexed entry/answer, `content_hash` = sha256 via `node:crypto`). `findDuplicateEntry()` in `rag.ts` is checked in `/api/ingest` BEFORE the Gemini call — resubmitting identical text/URL now reuses the existing entry (200 + `duplicate:true` + friendly message, `xp:0`) instead of creating a duplicate `entries`/`knowledge_chunks`/`questions` row every time (a real bug that existed until now). `upsertDocument()` (private) makes `indexContent()` idempotent: re-indexing the same `entryId` deletes-then-replaces its chunks rather than duplicating. Partial unique index scopes the hash constraint to `source='entry' and status='active'` so answer chunks (which can legitimately repeat) aren't affected. - **Alias-based index versioning:** `knowledge_chunks.index_version` + per-user `rag_index_pointers(user_id, active_version)` — the "alias" a reindex flips atomically. New ingests always tag chunks with the CURRENT `getActiveIndexVersion()`. `POST /api/rag/reindex` (new route, self-serve/RLS-only, no service-role key) builds a new generation in the background (`reindexDocument()`, paginated 200 docs/call, safe to call repeatedly) and only swaps the pointer + prunes the retired version once a full pass leaves zero pending documents — old version keeps serving every live retrieval throughout. Infrastructure for a future need (chunk-size/embedding-dim changes); nothing triggers it automatically today. - **Hybrid search:** `match_knowledge_chunks_hybrid` SQL function — Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) between vector kNN and Postgres `tsvector`/`ts_rank_cd` keyword search (the standard BM25 stand-in; true BM25 isn't native to Postgres). Query lexemes are OR'd (not AND'd like `plainto_tsquery`/`websearch_to_tsquery`) since the real caller passes a ~2000-char grounding excerpt, not a short phrase. `retrieveContext()` tries hybrid first, falls back to the (now version-aware) plain-vector `match_knowledge_chunks` via the existing `isMissingRag()` catch if the new SQL hasn't been applied yet. - **Observability:** `rag_retrieval_logs` (chunk-level attribution: query, mode, index_version, per-chunk id/similarity/rank as jsonb — written by `retrieveContext()`, `entry_id` backfilled via `linkRetrievalLogToEntry()` once the ingest's entry exists, since retrieval runs before the entry does). `getRetrievalMetrics()` aggregates recent logs in JS (avg similarity, zero-hit rate, by-mode/by-day breakdown). `rag_index_version_correlation()` SQL function + `getIndexVersionCorrelation()` wrapper join retrieval logs → grounded topics → `reviews.score` by `index_version` — **explicitly correlation, not causal regression detection** (several steps removed: retrieval quality → extraction → question quality → the user's own recall). **No new UI** — these are queryable diagnostics only, matching how this app has no admin dashboard anywhere. - `knowledge_chunks` table + HNSW cosine index + both match functions + `rag_documents`/`rag_index_pointers`/`rag_retrieval_logs`, all RLS-scoped to `auth.uid()`.
## Key modules - `src/components/BrainScene.tsx` (~850 lines) — the crown jewel. Stylised brain as particle shell; topics = glowing cortex patches; selection dives camera inside; label system: canvas-texture sprites in Space Grotesk, per-frame **screen-space declutter** (priority place → vertical nudge via `sprite.center` → fade), staggered edge labels, **labels are click targets** (raycast before glows, only when opacity > 0.3). Yaw bias +0.32 on landscape keeps selection out from under the info card. - `src/components/ProgressMap.tsx` — streak Momentum card + **streak-milestone progress bar** (ladder 3/7/14/21/30/50/75/100/150/200/365; "N days to a M-day streak" + record-based motivational line) + 16-week review activity heatmap. - `src/components/ProgressCalendar.tsx` — month-view study calendar (days lit by review count, month nav, today ring). **Lit days are buttons** → modal with that day's full report card via `getDayReport()`. Rendered only after profile loads to dodge SSR date mismatch. - `src/components/ReportCardView.tsx` — shared report-card renderer (score ring header + per-question cards incl. truefalse/multi option highlighting, partial-credit chip). Used by /recall report phase AND the calendar day modal. Exports `KIND_LABEL`. - `src/components/Footer.tsx` — global footer in `layout.tsx` (brand + kno·vis meaning, Explore links, Connect: /about, mailto contact, LinkedIn). - `getDayReport(day)` in data.ts — merges ALL graded `quiz_sessions` reports of a day (fallback: rebuild from `reviews` rows); `getLatestReportToday()` delegates to it, so "See report" shows every question asked today. `getTopicQuestions(topicId)` returns the bank WITH answers for the blog Q&A section (`select *` so a missing correct_indices column degrades gracefully). - `src/components/Nav.tsx` — top nav (Today/Brain/Add/Review + Profile/Sign-in pill; mobile 5th tab). Includes `authStatus` logic to correctly display 'Sign in' when unauthenticated in production. - `src/lib/text.ts` — `stripMarkdown()`; Gemini sometimes emits markdown and daily plans are cached in `daily_plans`, so it's applied both in `/api/plan` before saving AND at render time on the dashboard. - `src/components/RichText.tsx` — inline-markdown renderer (**bold**/*italic*/`code` → styled spans). Used for the dashboard "The thread today" insight instead of stripping (preserves emphasis). Recursive first-match parser; strips leading `#`/bullets. - `src/app/recall/page.tsx` (renamed from `review/` 2026-07-20) — reads `?topic=<id>` from `window.location.search`; single-task mode when matched (`singleTask` flag suppresses `markPlanCompleted`). Full-plan mode now includes ALL plan items (not just remaining): done items show an "Already reviewed today" phase and auto-advance; quiz session started only for pending (non-done) topic IDs. - `src/app/blogs/page.tsx` — standalone topic-blog library page. Filterable by search and category chip. Cards show mastery bar + review count. Links to `/blogs/[id]`. Added as **Blogs** (BookIcon) in `Nav.tsx` LINKS between Brain and Add. - `src/lib/dsa/` — **The Pattern Atlas content module** (added 2026-07-19): `types.ts` (DsaTopic/DsaProblem, `DSA_DIFFICULTY_COLORS`), `topics/*.ts` (18 files, one per NeetCode-150 chapter — all 150 question blogs as markdown-in-TS data, verified against live neetcode.io incl. current grouping: Stack=6, Backtracking=10), `index.ts` (ordered `DSA_TOPICS` registry + linear-nav helpers `dsaReadingOrder`/`dsaNeighbors`/`dsaProblemNumber`/`dsaStopHref`). Pure static data → SSG pages, zero AI/DB. Bodies render via `Markdown.tsx` inside `.article-body`. To add/edit content: edit the topic file; routes pick it up automatically. **Full hellointerview-style rewrite complete (2026-07-21):** every one of the 150 question bodies follows a `**Signal.**` / `**Brute force.**` / `**Optimal approach.**` / `viz:*` diagram / `**Complexity.**` / `**Thread.**` structure (was patchy prose with only 44/150 diagrams before); the four chapters still on an older ASCII-art/Python-fence template (`two-pointers.ts`, `binary-search.ts`, `dp-1d.ts`, `dp-2d.ts`) were normalized to house style in the same pass. Some chapter files also carry a hardcoded `questions: [...]` quiz array per problem (added by a separate process, unrelated to the story content — leave it alone when editing bodies). - `src/lib/notes.ts` — **personal notes, localStorage-backed** (`knovis.notes.v1`), deliberately outside the graph/Supabase. CRUD + `childrenOf`/`countDescendants` tree helpers; `ensureSeeded()` writes a one-time welcome note; every write dispatches `NOTES_EVENT` (+ native `storage` event) so the /notes page and the dashboard "Personal notes" section stay in sync. `Note` type in `types.ts` (`parent_id` = subnote nesting). - `src/components/Markdown.tsx` — dependency-free block+inline markdown renderer (headings, lists, blockquote, fenced code, hr, bold/italic/code/links). Used by the notes Read view. (`RichText.tsx` is the smaller inline-only variant for the dashboard insight.) **Inline math (added 2026-07-21):** a `$…$` inline rule → `renderMath()` renders the LaTeX-ish math that pervades the DSA/SQL bodies (`$O(N^2)$`, `$O(1)$`, `$\text{complement}=\text{target}-x$`, `$j>i$`) without pulling in KaTeX — ~60 command→Unicode maps, `^`/`_`→real `<sup>`/`<sub>`, `\text{}`/`\mathrm{}` upright vs italic variables, `\frac{a}{b}`→a⁄b; a `looksLikeMath()` guard (≤60 chars, no currency word/₹, needs a math operator/command OR no spaces) stops the Competition Act's real `$1 billion`/`$3 billion` currency from being eaten as math. **`viz:*` diagram fences** (added 2026-07-20): a fence tagged `viz:<kind>` (e.g. ` ```viz:array `) dispatches its JSON body to `src/components/viz/VizBlock.tsx` instead of rendering as code — `ArrayViz` (stepper), `TreeViz`, `TableDiffViz`, `FlowViz` primitives, shape-validated by `viz/types.ts`. `Markdown` takes `strictViz`/`vizAccent` props: the 10 hand-authored story-content call sites (DSA/SQL/Macro/SARFAESI/Competition-Act) pass `strictViz` so a malformed payload throws and fails the SSG build; the notes page (user-generated content) doesn't, so a bad fence there renders an inline error card instead of crashing. **Full rollout complete (2026-07-20):** all 34 DSA/SQL/Macro chapter files now have diagrams (91 total: 44 DSA, 41 SQL, 6 Macro) plus an end-to-end accuracy pass (8 real content errors found and fixed) — see SESSION_LOG for the per-file breakdown. `scripts/validate-viz.mts` fast-validates every `viz:*` payload in a set of files without a full Next build (reuses the real `parseVizPayload()` validator). - `getTopicSource(topicId)` in `data.ts` → `TopicSource` (`{kind:"url",url}` | `{kind:"text"}` | null): the blog page shows where a topic came from. Real mode joins `entry_topics`→`entries.source_url`; demo uses `demoTopicSource` (a few arxiv/github URLs, rest text). - `src/lib/srs.ts` — SM-2 spaced repetition (mastery calculations disabled). `src/lib/progress.ts` — `advanceStreak()` (renamed from `awardXp` 2026-07-16 when XP was removed entirely — streaks and active days, no XP field anywhere). - **RAG tables** — Run `supabase/schema-rag.sql` (creates `vector` extension, `knowledge_chunks` table + HNSW index + `match_knowledge_chunks` function, RLS). Safe to re-run. Until it's run, RAG is dormant and the app behaves exactly as before. - **Quiz DB tables** — Run `supabase/schema-quiz-tables-only.sql` in your Supabase SQL editor (this file avoids policy conflicts if your database already has policies): creates `questions` (bank: kind/options/correct_index/**correct_indices jsonb**/model_answer/difficulty/times_asked), `facts`, `quiz_sessions` (items snapshot + report jsonb), `quiz_answers` (unique session_id+question_index), with RLS policies and indexes. Both schema files include `alter table questions add column if not exists correct_indices jsonb` for older DBs. Alternatively, run the full `supabase/schema.sql` if starting fresh. - Quiz types in `types.ts`: `QuestionKind` (open|quickfire|mcq|truefalse|multi), `SessionQuestion` (client-safe, no answers), `QuizSession`, `ReportItem` (+selected_indices/correct_indices), `ReportCard`, `TopicQuestion` (bank WITH answers, for blogs), `DailyFact`; `Flashcard` type removed; `ExtractionResult` has `questions` + `facts` (no flashcards). - **Guided tour (added 2026-07-15, interactivity + review step 2026-07-15):** `src/lib/tour.ts` (14-step script incl. cross-page steps + a fully self-contained `custom: "review-demo"` step with its own made-up question `DEMO_REVIEW_QUESTION` + storage helpers: seen flag in localStorage `knovis.tour.seen.v1`, active state in sessionStorage `knovis.tour.state.v1` so it survives the guest-mode hard navigations) + `src/components/TutorialTour.tsx` (mounted once in `layout.tsx`; dim overlay + glowing spotlight cut-out via giant box-shadow, translucent blurred `.tour-card` in globals.css, progress dots, Back/Next/skip, Esc/arrow keys; polls `[data-tour="…"]` anchors every 150 ms so late-loading client data & route changes are handled, picks the visible anchor when desktop/mobile share one, centered fallback if an anchor never appears). Anchors: `nav` (Nav desktop+mobile), `plan`/`recall-cta`/`momentum`/`calendar`/`fact`/`notes` (dashboard), `brain-canvas`/`brain-search` (/brain), `blogs-intro` (/blogs), `add-form` (/add). **Flows:** first browser visit → login page arms mode "first" + guest cookie → tour runs over demo pages → complete/skip clears guest cookie → `/login`; every "Continue as guest" click arms mode "demo" → tour replays → finish stays in demo. TutorialTour also self-arms once for unseen visitors already in demo mode (abandoned mid-tour in another tab). - **Spotlighted features stay genuinely interactive** (calendar day → real report-card modal, brain canvas/search, add-form inputs): the overlay wrapper is `pointer-events:none` whenever a spotlight target exists (only `.tour-card` re-enables `pointer-events:auto` on itself via CSS), so clicks fall straight through to the real page. `ProgressCalendar`'s day-report modal was bumped to `z-[125]` (was `z-50`) so it renders above the tour's `z-120` dim layer instead of being hidden by it. - **Link navigation is blocked globally while the tour is active** (capture-phase `document` click listener in TutorialTour checks `target.closest("a")` and calls `preventDefault`/`stopPropagation`) so a stray click on the Nav, a plan row, a blog card, or "Read the full topic" can't route the visitor away mid-tour — non-anchor interactions (buttons, canvas, inputs) are untouched. Opt out per-element with a `data-allow-nav` ancestor (used by the /add demo-gate popup's "Sign in" link). - **"Try a review" step** (`custom: "review-demo"`, inserted right after "One-tap review"): a self-contained mock quiz rendered directly inside `.tour-card` (`ReviewDemoCard` in TutorialTour.tsx) — never touches real quiz state/Supabase. Pick an option → Submit → mocked report card (score ring via `BigScoreRing`, feedback, correct answer) → Continue. - `src/lib/demo.ts` — demo-mode seed data. `src/lib/data.ts` — data access. - `src/lib/types.ts` — `Topic`, `TopicLink`, `categoryColor(category)`. - `src/lib/supabase/{client,server}.ts`, `src/lib/gemini.ts`. - `src/proxy.ts` — Next 16 proxy (migrated from middleware.ts). Auth gate honours the `knovis_guest` cookie (guest/demo access without an account) and deletes it as soon as a real session exists. - **Guest mode / isDemo:** `isDemo` (demo.ts) = no `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` OR `knovis_guest=1` cookie. Cookie helpers `enableGuestMode()`/`clearGuestMode()`. Guest→signed-in transitions use full page loads so the module-level constant re-evaluates. Render-time isDemo checks (Nav, /add, /profile) go through post-mount state to keep hydration stable. - **Plan done-tracking:** `PlanItem.done` computed at read time (today's `reviews` rows in real mode; `demoState.done` in demo). Dashboard plan section: green dots = remaining, done rows dimmed/✓/last, 5 rows + "Show all" toggle (plan caps at 10 items). Full /recall runs cover only remaining items.
## Security posture (reviewed 2026-07-15, hardened 2026-07-18) - **SSRF guard on link ingest, DNS-rebinding-safe (`src/app/api/ingest/route.ts`, fixed 2026-07-18):** `assertPublicHttpUrl()` is only the cheap string-level pre-filter (protocol, credentials, port, literal private IPs, `.local`/`.internal`). The REAL boundary is `ssrfSafeDispatcher` — a custom `undici` `Agent` whose `connect.lookup` resolves DNS itself, rejects the request if ANY resolved address is loopback/private/link-local/CGNAT, and only then hands the validated IP to the actual TCP connect (same lookup call does both, so there's no second, unvalidated DNS query for an attacker's server to answer differently). Closes the bypass where a hostname string looks harmless but its DNS record points at `169.254.169.254`/`127.0.0.1`/internal ranges — the old hostname-only check couldn't catch that. TLS SNI/cert hostname validation is unaffected (Node derives those from the URL, not from what `lookup` returns). Verified against real DNS-rebinding test domains (`*.nip.io`, which resolves to whatever IP is embedded in the subdomain) — metadata/loopback/private targets all blocked, real public HTTPS sites (incl. TLS) still work. - **Per-user daily Gemini-call caps, split 9 + 1 (`INGEST_DAILY_LIMIT`=9, `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT`=1, added 2026-07-18):** ingest capped at 9/day (down from an unbounded-in-practice 20); quiz AI grading (`gradeSession()`) separately capped at 1/day via a new `quiz_sessions.ai_graded` flag — deliberately NOT shared with the ingest budget, so a heavy ingest day can never fully starve the review flow. Exceeding the quiz cap never blocks finishing a session — it silently reuses the existing heuristic (keyword-overlap) grader, the same fallback already used when Gemini itself errors. Both day-boundaries now use the CLIENT's local timezone (`clampTz`/`localTodayForOffset`/`dayStartUtcIso` from `dates.ts`) — the ingest cap previously reset at the server's own midnight (effectively UTC on Vercel), not the user's. Hitting the ingest cap returns `resetAt` (ISO instant of the user's next local midnight) in the 429 body; `/add` shows a popup ("You can add more starting tomorrow at —") instead of just inline error text, via a new `ApiError` class in `data.ts` that carries the HTTP status so the UI can branch on 429 without parsing message text. - **`PUBLIC_PATHS` exact/segment match, not prefix match** (`src/proxy.ts`, fixed 2026-07-18): was `path.startsWith(p)`, which would treat a hypothetical future route like `/login-help` as public just for sharing a text prefix with `/login`. Now `path === p || path.startsWith(p + "/")`. - All API routes wrap `request.json()` in try/catch (malformed-body → 400). `/auth/callback` redirects to `/login?error=auth` when `exchangeCodeForSession` fails instead of silently landing on the dashboard. - RLS: every table `own <x>` policy is `auth.uid() = user_id` (profiles: `= id`; entry_topics via entry ownership). data.ts queries Supabase directly from the browser — RLS is the only thing isolating users, so keep it on every new table (the new `knowledge_chunks` has it). - **Ingest guardrails (added 2026-07-16, fixed 2026-07-16, `src/lib/guardrails.ts`):** `looksLikeGibberish()` — dependency-free heuristics (repeated-pattern runs, meaningful-char ratio, distinct-word ratio) reject obvious spam/keyboard-mashing before it burns a Gemini call or pollutes the knowledge base; deliberately lenient (dense technical text, code, LaTeX, non-English prose all pass — tested against a case suite). **Two false-positive bugs fixed same day** (found via a real link-ingest of an external blog post that got wrongly rejected): (1) the repeated-pattern-flood regex ran on ANY 10+ char repeat including pure punctuation, so ASCII fraction bars/table borders/box-drawing dividers common in technical writing tripped it — now requires the repeated unit to contain a letter/digit; (2) the distinct-character-ratio check (since removed entirely) was not scale-invariant — English has only 36 possible alnum characters, so ANY real prose past ~2 sentences (~300 chars) mathematically saturates the alphabet and its ratio collapses below the threshold regardless of legitimacy; its intended purpose was already redundantly covered by the flood-regex and the distinct-word-ratio check, so it was dropped rather than re-tuned. `sanitizeField()` strips control chars and clamps length, applied to EVERY field `extractKnowledge()` returns (title, summary, topic name/summary/key_points, connection reason, question prompt/model_answer/options, fact text) before DB insert; topic `category` is validated against `CATEGORY_COLORS` keys (`src/lib/types.ts`) with a `"General"` fallback — previously unvalidated. **Prompt-injection hardening** in `extractKnowledge()` (`gemini.ts`): the pasted/fetched text and the RAG grounding block are explicitly framed as "untrusted data, not instructions" with a rule telling the model to never follow embedded commands (relevant since link ingestion feeds arbitrary third-party webpage text into the prompt). Blast radius if a prompt injection ever succeeded is contained — no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` anywhere in the app (verified by grep), so AI-generated text can only ever render as inert text via React's default escaping, with one exception below. - **Markdown link scheme validation** (`src/components/Markdown.tsx`, used by `/notes`): `isSafeHref()` only renders `[text](url)` as a clickable `<a>` when the scheme is `http`/`https`/`mailto` (or scheme-less/relative) — a `javascript:`/`data:` URI now renders as inert plain text instead of an executable link. Verified live: a `javascript:` and a `data:text/html,<script>` link both render as non-clickable text while a normal `https://` link still works.
## Dates / "today" (fixed 2026-07-15) - `src/lib/dates.ts` is the single source for day boundaries. The app's "day" is the USER'S LOCAL calendar day, never the server's UTC day. Client passes its tz offset (`new Date().getTimezoneOffset()`) to `/api/plan?tz=` and quiz finish/complete bodies; server uses `localTodayForOffset`/`dayStartUtcIso`/`clampTz`. Client bucketing (calendar, heatmap, review detail) uses `localDayKey(new Date(created_at))`. This was the root cause of "answered questions show as not attempted": a UTC-vs-local day mismatch (e.g. evening IST reviews landing on the next UTC date) plus done-tracking that only read `reviews`. `doneTopicsToday()` now unions `reviews` rows AND `topics.last_reviewed_at` (always written on finish) so a failed reviews insert can't strand items.
## Conventions / gotchas - Dev server: use `.claude/launch.json` name `synapse-dev` (autoPort; 3000 often taken by other projects). - **Recall page** (`src/app/recall/page.tsx`, renamed from "Review" 2026-07-20 — `/review` permanently redirects to `/recall`): reads `?topic=` via `useSearchParams` (Suspense-wrapped) so switching between `/recall?topic=A`, `?topic=B` and `/recall` re-runs the init effect while the page stays mounted (fixes the "clicking a different question reroutes to the old one" bug — the old `window.location.search` read only ran on first mount). Full-plan runs sort pending-first so the session never opens on an "already reviewed" card; a single deep-linked task that turns out to be the LAST pending topic now marks the day complete itself. - **Demo state persists** to `localStorage` (`knovis.demo.v1`) so guest progress survives reloads/hard-navigations: reviews/reports/streak carry over; `done` flags + `completed` reset on a new local day (mirrors the real backend). `saveDemoState()` after every demo mutation; `loadDemoState()` on module init when `isDemo`. - Knowledge graph of this repo lives in `graphify-out/` (`graph.json`); query it instead of grepping. **The graphify CLI is installed** (PyPI `graphifyy`, double-y) at `/Users/bucc/.local/bin/graphify` — if not on PATH in a fresh shell, call it by full path or `export PATH="/Users/bucc/.local/bin:$PATH"`. Use `graphify query "…"` / `path` / `explain`; run `graphify update .` after code changes (AST-only, no API cost). NB: an unrelated `graphify` npm package (a "Random Graph Generator") is in the global npm dir with no bin — ignore it. - Verify visual changes in the browser (demo mode works logged-out). - **Notes are localStorage-only** (per-browser, no server/graph). Cross-device sync would need a Supabase `notes` table — deliberately deferred (see TASKS backlog). - `npx tsc --noEmit` for typecheck; no test suite yet.

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Session Log
> Milestone journal, newest first. One short entry per completed milestone. Keep entries terse — this file is read at the start of every session.
## 2026-07-21 — DSA: full hellointerview-style rewrite of all 150 questions COMPLETE (diagrams + rewrite, sequentially by hand) - **Why:** user checked the 2026-07-20 `viz:*` rollout and found most of the 150 individual DSA question blogs still read as plain prose with no diagram — the rollout had added 44 diagrams total (spread across chapter intros + a minority of problems), not one per question. Audited via `DSA_TOPICS` import (grep undercounts — body strings use escaped backticks): confirmed 150 problems, only 44 (29%) had a `viz:*` diagram. First response was a checklist-only pass (`DSA_HELLOINTERVIEW_CHECKLIST.md`, still in the repo as the permanent per-question tracking record). User then asked for the actual rewrite, explicitly **one question at a time, no parallel agents** — this content is the site's main selling point. - **New target structure, applied to all 150 bodies:** `**Signal.**` (the interview tell) / `**Brute force.**` (the naive approach + its complexity, named honestly even when the "brute force" is a lesser-known alternative like greedy-that-fails) / `**Optimal approach.**` (the real solution) / a `viz:*` diagram / `**Complexity.**` (with an explicit "versus the brute force" comparison) / `**Thread.**` (unchanged house touch — the link to the next problem). Replaces the old 6-beat prose shape (*The problem. → The naive move. → The insight. → The walk-through. → Complexity. → The thread.*) chapters 1-11 used, and outright replaces the older ASCII-art/Python-code-fence template that `two-pointers.ts`, `binary-search.ts`, `dp-1d.ts`, `dp-2d.ts` were still on (normalized to house style in the same pass, folding in the backlog item that had flagged this separately). - **Execution:** went chapter by chapter through all 18 `src/lib/dsa/topics/*.ts` files, editing every problem body by hand (`Edit` for files with existing content worth preserving structure from, `Write` for full-file rewrites when safe). Diagrams used all four `viz:*` kinds — `array` (most common: pointer-stepper walkthroughs), `tree` (recursion/BFS/trie structures), `table-diff` (2-D DP tables, before/after comparisons), `flow` (grid/graph node-edge layouts) — chosen per problem's actual shape rather than defaulting to one kind. - **Ran into and preserved a concurrent change:** partway through (after ~5 chapters), a separate process (committed as `b9a158f Add hardcoded questions to all rewritten DSA chapters`) added a hardcoded `questions: [{kind, prompt, options, correct_index, model_answer, difficulty}, ...]` quiz array to every problem in the chapters already rewritten at that point, plus `types.ts`. This is unrelated content (quiz questions, not story prose) living in the same file. Detected it via a system-reminder diff on `binary-search.ts` and adjusted: for every remaining chapter, checked for a `questions` field first (`grep -c 'kind:'`) and used surgical `Edit` calls scoped to just the `body` string (never `Write`) whenever one was present, so no `questions` array was ever touched or lost. Confirmed post-hoc that every chapter's `questions` blocks still parse (`kind:` grep count matched problem count) after its body rewrite. - **Final tally: 150/150 problems now have exactly one `viz:*` diagram each** (was 44/150), all with the new 5-header structure. Per-chapter diagram counts: Arrays&Hashing 9, Two Pointers 5, Sliding Window 6, Stack 6, Binary Search 7, Linked List 11, Trees 15, Heap 7, Backtracking 10, Tries 3, Graphs 13, Advanced Graphs 6, 1-D DP 12, 2-D DP 11, Greedy 8, Intervals 6 (was the worst-covered chapter, 0/6 before), Math & Geometry 8, Bit Manipulation 7. - **Verification, continuous:** ran `npx tsx scripts/validate-viz.mts <file>` and `npx tsc --noEmit` after every single chapter (18/18 clean, one at a time — never batched to the end). Final full-project pass: `npx tsx scripts/validate-viz.mts src/lib/dsa/topics/*.ts` → "150 block(s) checked — all valid"; `npx tsc --noEmit` clean; full `npm run build` clean, all 150 question pages + 18 chapter pages present in the static output. - **`DSA_HELLOINTERVIEW_CHECKLIST.md`** (created this session, kept — not a scratch file) now has every `[D]`/`[R]` box checked per problem, chapter-done timestamps, and a summary banner at the bottom. Chapter intros (18 of them) were deliberately left as prose-only, no diagram — the user's ask and this whole pass were scoped to "questions," not chapter-level essays; that remains an open, undecided item if wanted later.
## 2026-07-20 — Story content full rollout COMPLETE: 91 diagrams + 8 accuracy fixes across all 34 chapters (3rd of the 4-part redesign, done) - **Finished the rollout** started in the previous entry — the remaining 20 files (17 not-started + 3 partial) were completed via a 2nd resume batch of 7 background agents (DSA-R1..R4, SQL-R1/R2, MACRO-R1), hitting the account session-limit once more mid-batch but with zero corruption (same verify-before-resume discipline as before). All 7 finished clean on the retry. - **New tracking doc, now retired:** `VIZ_ROLLOUT_CHECKLIST.md` was created specifically so a session-limit interruption wouldn't require re-deriving the whole mechanism/status from conversation context — agents were pointed at it instead of getting the full mechanism explanation inline, and it doubled as the per-file done/partial/todo checklist. Deleted now that the rollout is 100% done (this entry is the permanent record). - **Final tally — 91 `viz:*` diagrams across all 34 chapter files** (18 DSA incl. pilot, 12 SQL incl. pilot, 6 Macro incl. pilot; SARFAESI and the paused Competition Act were out of scope for this pass): 44 in DSA (`viz:array`/`viz:tree`), 41 in SQL (`viz:table-diff`, several reusing real LeetCode example rows for fidelity), 6 in Macro (`viz:flow`, exactly 1 per chapter as targeted). - **8 real accuracy errors found and fixed while agents read every file end-to-end** (not just spot-checks): DSA `binary-search.ts` (Median of Two Sorted Arrays walkthrough used the even-length formula on an odd-length example), `dp-2d.ts` ×2 (Interleaving String table had a cell mismarked ✓ instead of ✗; Edit Distance backtrace path was arithmetically inconsistent with its own dp table), `greedy.ts` (Valid Parenthesis String walkthrough claimed the failure happened "on the second )" when it's actually the first), `sliding-window.ts` ×2 (Best Time to Buy/Sell Stock walkthrough mislabeled price values as day indices; Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters claimed the wrong eviction pair sequence — hand-traced and corrected); SQL `common-table-expressions.ts` (intro/thread text promised recursive CTEs the chapter's 3 problems never actually use — reworded to match real content); Macro `conceptualising-the-macroeconomy.ts` (wrong publication date for Quesnay's *Tableau Économique*). Everything else read was independently verified correct (dozens of worked examples, dates, and technical claims checked and left unchanged). - **Verification, every batch:** `git status --short` (confirm only assigned files touched, no corruption from any mid-response failure), `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npx tsx scripts/validate-viz.mts <every touched file>` (91/91 blocks valid, zero FAILs across the whole series), and a full `npm run build` (clean, 331+ pages) run after the final batch. This is stronger than trusting individual agent self-reports — every claim was independently re-checked against the actual files on disk. - **Noted, not fixed (flagged for a separate decision):** `two-pointers.ts`, `dp-1d.ts`, and `dp-2d.ts` use an older content template (ASCII-art diagrams, Python code fences) that predates the prose-only house style the rest of DSA uses — `viz:*` diagrams were added additively without rewriting these, per the "additive, not a rewrite" scope. Also spawned a separate background task earlier for a pre-existing, unrelated `***bold-italic***` Markdown rendering gap (not part of this rollout). - **Competition Act:** stays paused from `/blogs` discovery (from the earlier pilot session) — not touched in this rollout, out of scope per the user's original ask.
## 2026-07-20 — Story content full rollout: 10/33 chapters done, 3 partial (session-limit interrupted, in-progress) - **Why:** after the pilot (see entry below) was approved, kicked off the full accuracy + `viz:*` rollout across the remaining 33 chapter files (17 DSA, 11 SQL, 5 Macro), parallelized via 11 background agents grouped by content volume (~800-1000 lines each). - **Hit the account session-usage limit twice** (once at launch, once ~2/3 through the retry batch) — an external API constraint, not a code issue. Each time, **verified via `git status` that zero files were left partially corrupted**: agents either complete a file's edits atomically or die before writing anything for it. After the 2nd interruption, additionally ran `npx tsc --noEmit` (clean) and `npx tsx scripts/validate-viz.mts` across every touched file (40/40 `viz:*` blocks valid, zero FAILs) and a full `npm run build` (clean, all 331 pages) to positively confirm no corruption before reporting status — this is stronger verification than just trusting agent self-reports. - **Fully complete (10 files):** DSA `dp-1d.ts` (4 diagrams — Min Cost Climbing Stairs/House Robber/Coin Change/Partition Equal Subset Sum DP-fill walkthroughs), `binary-search.ts` (3 diagrams + **1 accuracy fix**: the Median of Two Sorted Arrays walkthrough used the even-length `max(A_left,B_left)` formula on an odd-length example where `min(A_right,B_right)` was required — verified the true median against the merged array and corrected it), `tries.ts` (2 tree diagrams), `stack.ts` (3 array-as-stack-trace diagrams). SQL `advanced-joins-and-set-ops.ts`/`sorting-and-limiting.ts`/`case-when.ts`/`string-and-date-functions.ts` (12 table-diffs total, several reusing real LeetCode example rows for fidelity), `aggregation.ts`, `basic-joins.ts`. Macro `money-and-interest-rates.ts` (1 flow diagram: RBI repo rate → bank rates → asset markets → real investment/consumption, the monetary transmission mechanism), `macroeconomic-policy.ts` (1 flow diagram: the Paradox of Thrift's individual-vs-system-logic chain). - **Partial, left in a safe state:** DSA `graphs.ts` (2 diagrams landed; group-mates `linked-list.ts`/`backtracking.ts` not started yet). SQL `select-and-filter.ts` (2 of ~5 planned diagrams). Macro `conceptualising-the-macroeconomy.ts` (1 accuracy fix landed — corrected Quesnay's *Tableau Économique* publication date — diagram not yet added; agent had also independently verified Joan Robinson's actual 1937 term "beggar-my-neighbour", Kalecki's co-credit for effective demand, and Solow/Romer/Leontief/Copeland dates as all correct, no other changes needed there). - **Not yet started:** DSA `dp-2d.ts`, `two-pointers.ts`, `trees.ts`, `sliding-window.ts`, `linked-list.ts`, `backtracking.ts`, `greedy.ts`, `math-geometry.ts`, `heap.ts`, `bit-manipulation.ts`, `advanced-graphs.ts`; SQL `window-functions-analytics.ts`, `data-transformation.ts`, `subqueries.ts`, `common-table-expressions.ts`; Macro `output-and-employment.ts`, `economic-growth.ts`.
