See TASKS.md for the exact resume list. - **New tooling:** `scripts/validate-viz.mts` — reuses the real production `parseVizPayload()` validator (no full Next build needed) to fast-check every `viz:*` block in a given set of files; used by every content agent as a self-check and by the orchestrating session for final verification. - **Noted, not fixed:** `two-pointers.ts` and `dp-1d.ts` (and likely `dp-2d.ts`, not yet confirmed) predate the current prose-only house style — older template with ASCII-art diagrams and Python code fences. Left untouched per "additive, not a rewrite"; flagged for a separate normalization decision later.
## 2026-07-20 — Story content: `viz:*` diagram system + pilot (3rd of the 4-part redesign, in-progress) - **Why:** story series (DSA/SQL/Macroeconomics) are prose + code-fence only, no diagrams — user wants them more engaging while keeping the zero-AI, hand-authored-static-content architecture. Competition Act paused from discovery per user request (content/routes untouched, just not linked). Design approach was Fable-5-advised during the plan: custom `viz:*` Markdown fences dispatching to a small SVG primitive library, authored inline in the existing `body: string` fields — no schema change. - **Competition Act paused:** its entry in `STORY_SERIES` (`src/app/blogs/page.tsx`) commented out (not deleted) — route/data/seed machinery (`src/lib/competition-act/*`, `/blogs/competition-act/*` routes) all still intact for anyone who already started it; just not discoverable from the library page anymore. - **`viz:*` Markdown extension:** `src/components/Markdown.tsx`'s fence handler now captures the info-string language tag; a fence tagged `viz:<kind>` (e.g. ` ```viz:array `) dispatches its JSON body to `VizBlock` instead of rendering as a code block. New `src/components/viz/`: `types.ts` (payload shapes + `parseVizPayload()` shape validation, throws descriptively on any problem), `ArrayViz.tsx` (client component — stepper through array/pointer/highlight frames, for DSA walkthroughs), `TreeViz.tsx` (BFS-level-layout node/edge diagram, static SVG), `TableDiffViz.tsx` (index-aligned before/after row tables with added/removed/changed highlighting, for SQL), `FlowViz.tsx` (explicit row/col node grid + labeled directed edges, for Macro concept/flow diagrams), `VizBlock.tsx` (dispatcher). - **Build-fails-loudly guarantee, safe for user content:** `Markdown` gained `strictViz`/`vizAccent` props. All 10 hand-authored story-content `<Markdown>` call sites (DSA/SQL/Macro/SARFAESI/Competition-Act chapter intros + problem/section bodies) now pass `strictViz` (malformed payload throws → fails the Next.js SSG build for that page) and `vizAccent={topic.color/chapter.color}`. The one user-generated-content call site (`src/app/notes/page.tsx`) intentionally does NOT pass `strictViz` — a bad `viz:` fence in a personal note renders an inline "⚠ invalid diagram" card instead of crashing the page. **Verified the guarantee directly:** deliberately corrupted a pilot payload's JSON and confirmed `npm run build` failed with a precise error naming the exact page and JSON parse position; reverted, confirmed clean build again. - **Pilot content (3 chapters, one per series, the same ones spot-checked for accuracy during planning):** `arrays-hashing.ts` "Contains Duplicate" gets a 4-frame `viz:array` walkthrough (bag-insertion pointer stepping through `[1,2,3,1]`, final frame highlighting the duplicate); `window-functions-ranking.ts` gets two `viz:table-diff` diagrams ("Rank Scores" — DENSE_RANK column appearing; "Department Highest Salary" — PARTITION BY-then-filter, showing a row dropped by the rank filter); `what-is-economics.ts` gets a 3-node `viz:flow` diagram for "The Economy as an Embedded System" (Ecology → embeds → Society → embeds → Economy). - **Verified:** `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean (331 pages). Browser-checked all 3 pilot pages in demo mode: DOM/text-level checks (screenshot renderer was flaky again this session, same documented issue as prior sessions) confirm each diagram's content and structure — array stepper shows the pointer moving, step counter, and clicking "Next →" actually advances the frame; table-diff shows correct before/after rows with the new column; flow diagram shows nodes and edge labels. No console errors on any of the 3 pages. - **Not done yet (paused at the plan's pilot checkpoint):** the remaining ~15 DSA chapters (147 problems), 11 SQL chapters (38 problems), and 5 Macro chapters still need their accuracy pass + visualizations. Per the plan's rollout order, this pauses here for a sign-off on the pattern before committing to the full-volume pass. - **Noticed, not fixed (pre-existing, out of scope):** `Markdown.tsx`'s inline parser doesn't handle `***bold italic***` (only `**bold**` and `*italic*` separately) — `what-is-economics.ts`'s `***ceteris paribus***` renders with a stray literal asterisk. Flagged as a separate small fix, not touched here to keep this milestone scoped to visualizations.
## 2026-07-20 — Rename "Review" → "Recall" (2nd of the 4-part redesign) - **Why:** "Review" undersold the feature — it's a graded active-recall quiz session, not passive re-reading. Fable-5-advised naming pick during the plan: "Recall" (names the actual cognitive act, works as noun/verb, avoids "Quiz"'s trivia connotation and "Practice"'s genericness). - **Route:** `src/app/review/page.tsx` → `src/app/recall/page.tsx` via `git mv` (component/function names inside — `ReviewPage`/`ReviewRunner`/`getTodayReviewDetail` — left as internal identifiers, not user-facing). Added a permanent redirect `/review` → `/recall` in `next.config.ts` for old links/bookmarks (query params like `?topic=`/`?series=` pass through automatically since they're not part of the source pattern). - **User-facing copy updated:** `Nav.tsx` pill "Review"→"Recall"; `Footer.tsx` explore link; dashboard (`src/app/page.tsx`) "Revision plan" heading → "Today's recall", CTA `data-tour` anchor `review-cta`→`recall-cta`, all `/review?topic=` deep links → `/recall?...`; `src/app/blogs/[id]/page.tsx` and `src/components/StoryLearnPanel.tsx` CTA links/labels ("Review →" → "Recall →"); guided tour (`src/lib/tour.ts`) step titles/body copy ("One-tap review"→"One-tap recall", "Try a review"→"Try a recall session", nav-overview line) and its `target`/`route` fields — the `custom: "review-demo"` step id itself was kept as an internal identifier. - **Deliberately unchanged (per plan):** the `reviews` DB table, `getReviews`/`getDayReport`/`doneTopicsToday`/`advanceStreak`, all SM-2/spaced-repetition "review" terminology (ProgressMap "Review activity", "Due for review today" plan reason text, "review rotation" copy in `StoryLearnPanel.tsx`) — these name the real spaced-repetition scheduling concept, a distinct thing from the feature's UI name. - **Verified:** `tsc --noEmit` (after clearing a stale `.next` route-type cache referencing the old path) + `npm run build` clean, `/recall` registered as a static route in the build output. Browser-checked in demo mode: nav pill reads "Recall" and highlights on `/recall`, dashboard shows "Today's recall" heading, navigating to `/review` redirects to `/recall`, a full 10-question recall session renders and works, no console errors.

Added a `dueSeriesSlugs` set (series with a non-done task today) passed to `StoryJourney` for its sort. - **`src/components/StoryJourney.tsx`:** caps the dashboard preview to the top 3 stories (due-today first, then most-recently-started via `started_at`), dropped the per-chapter bead trail (kept the radial ring), added an "All stories (N) →" footer link to `/blogs`. - **`src/app/blogs/page.tsx`:** the "Read as one story" module (already a scaling divider-row list) now floats started series to the top via a `sortedSeries` memo, stable-sorted so untouched series keep their original relative order. - **Verified:** `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean (150 DSA + 41 SQL + 17 Macro + other SSG paths all built). ## 2026-07-20 — Story agent prompts: fixed compile-breaking gaps, added coverage & depth rules - **Audit of all 26 `prompts/story-agents/*.md`** (per-domain agents that turn user-supplied material into `StoryChapter`/`StorySection` TS objects). Found the shared template had no rule forcing full coverage of the input, two schema gaps that would produce invalid TS (`multi` questions had no `correct_indices` example — only `correct_index` was ever shown; `difficulty` enum values were never stated, and this project has already shipped a real bug from an agent guessing `"easy"` instead of `"basic"`, see `b11c83f`), no depth/length target, and no engagement rules beyond the 5-step narrative skeleton. - **Patched all 26 files identically** (scripted via `node`, verified with grep + bracket-balance checks — no manual per-file edits, since the template is byte-identical outside domain-specific phrasing): added a "Coverage & Completeness" section (decompose the full input, split into multiple chapters instead of cutting material, self-check pass before returning), a "Content Depth & Engagement" section (300-600 word body target, hook-first, analogies, second-person voice, tables/lists where structural), a worked `multi`-question example with `correct_indices`, an explicit `difficulty: "basic" | "intermediate" | "advanced"` enum comment, a self-contained-facts rule (facts surface standalone as "fact of the day" cards), fixed a self-contradictory output-fence instruction, and added guidance comments for the previously-unexplained `chapter`/`prereqs`/`unlocks`/`sectionNumber`/`tagline` fields. - **Verified:** every file has both new sections, no leftover placeholder/contradictory text, every file's embedded TS block has balanced brackets and a clean `multi` example (no stray `correct_index`). Domain-specific pedagogical frameworks (tech's HelloInterview steps, medical/history/legal's 5-step narrative, etc.) left untouched.
## 2026-07-20 — Esc exits the dive-inside view - **`src/app/brain/page.tsx`:** window `keydown` listener — Escape clears the walked path (`setPath([])`, same as the info-card close button), zooming the camera back out. Skipped while an input/textarea has focus so search keeps its own Esc behaviour. Verified in browser: search → select "Bayes' Theorem" → dive-inside → Esc → card closes, camera settles back to overview.
## 2026-07-20 — Organic "thought waves": flood-fill activation replaces mechanical sine wave - **Problem:** the brain's idle wave was `sin(dist·0.45 − t·1.5)` from a fixed origin — a perfect concentric ripple on a loop; plus globally-synced shell breathing and metronome pulse dots. Felt mechanical. Reference: A* pathfinding on a real map (golden flood crawling the street network). - **New system (all in `src/components/BrainScene.tsx`):** - kNN graph (k=6, spatial-hash build) over the 7,500 shell particles; a wave = Dijkstra flood from a seed with ±28% jittered edge weights (ragged frontier) and 3.5× cost to cross the medial fissure (hemisphere-hugging spread). Per-particle arrival times → `aWave` vec2 buffer attribute (2 concurrent wave slots); brightness (sharp gold crest + ~2s ember tail + pre-arrival shimmer) computed entirely in the shell shader from `uWaveT/uWaveI` uniforms — zero per-frame CPU for the flood itself. - Lifecycle: ambient wave every 4–9s seeded at a random topic (extent 30–70%, randomized speed); ~1-in-6 a slow full-brain "deep breath" from the cerebellum; selection spawns a wave AT the clicked topic; ambient suppressed during story highlight & selection. Hidden-tab clock jumps shift wave starts so floods resume, not skip. - Chain reaction: topic nodes flare (`flashK` halo swell) the moment the front arrives; vein/link edges brighten in arrival order [tA→tB] then cool (replaces the radial `waveEdgeGlow`), veins blush gold as the front crosses. - De-mechanized: global `0.5+0.08·sin` shell breathing → per-particle hash-phased shimmer in-shader; idle pulse dots now ease along curves and rest 1–4.5s between runs (focused/route dots still flow continuously). - **Verified:** tsc clean; 200-topic demo in browser — waves spawn (logged), flood organically with ragged gold frontier + ember tail, cerebellum nodes flare on arrival, no console errors; brightness tuned (cap 1.15, gold ×0.75) so the crest never blows out to white. Note: Browser-pane tab reports `document.hidden=true` between captures, freezing rAF — verified via burst captures; runs continuously in a real tab.
## 2026-07-19 — Post-prod fixes: greeting, brain brightness, review headers, learned-section surfacing - **Greeting stuck on "Good morning" (`src/app/page.tsx`):** the page is prerendered and `suppressHydrationWarning` makes React KEEP the server (build/UTC) text, so the greeting/date never updated to the viewer's local time. Now computed from a `now` state set in `useEffect` (client-only), so a real re-render corrects it. Verified: "Good afternoon" at local 15:00. - **Brain too bright when filled (`src/components/BrainScene.tsx`):** many additive glows summed to a white blob. Toned down the per-node loop opacities (core 0.85→0.6, halo 0.4→0.24, cluster 0.9→0.55), the cluster colour boost (1.45→1.15), the core white-lerp (0.55→0.4), and the shell base (0.5→0.4). Clusters read as distinct nodes again. - **Review group headers (`src/app/review/page.tsx`):** the big `text-2xl` "General Knowledge" title looked off. Replaced with an on-brand `GroupHeader` (colour-coded dot + small uppercase label via `SERIES_COLORS`), and it's hidden entirely when there's only one group (so a lone "General Knowledge" title no longer sits awkwardly). - **Learned story section not appearing on home/review (`src/app/api/plan/route.ts`):** two causes. (1) The plan caps at 10 sorted most-overdue-first, so a just-learned section (due "now", 0 days overdue) was crowded out — now never-reviewed due topics are prioritised (newest-created first), so a freshly-learned section surfaces. (2) Once a plan is cached for the day it never picked up newly-due topics — the GET now rebuilds a non-completed cached plan if any topic became due after it was built. Reason label "New — ready for its first review" for never-reviewed items. - **Verified:** tsc + build clean; browser (demo) greeting/brain/review-single-group confirmed, no console errors. Deployed to prod. Signed-in multi-story surfacing not exercisable locally.

Replaced with ONE connected module (`src/app/blogs/page.tsx`): a single framed `glass` container with a shared header + "N series" chip, and the series as connected rows sharing the frame — hairline `divide-y` dividers, a per-series accent colour spine, tag chip, and meta.
