Also tightened `PUBLIC_PATHS` matching in `src/proxy.ts` from a raw prefix match (`path.startsWith(p)`) to exact-or-segment match (`path === p || path.startsWith(p + "/")`) — not currently exploitable (no route collides with `/login`/`/auth` today) but a real gap for any future route sharing that text prefix. - **Verified:** SSRF fix tested against real DNS-rebinding domains (`*.nip.io`, which resolves subdomains to the IP embedded in them) — `169.254.169.254.nip.io`, `127.0.0.1.nip.io`, `10.0.0.5.nip.io`, `192.168.1.1.nip.io` all correctly blocked; `example.com`/`en.wikipedia.org` (real HTTPS) still fetch correctly, proving TLS wasn't broken. `tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` clean after every change. Live-curled all 4 routes for auth bypass, method fuzzing (405 on wrong verb), and nonexistent routes (404) — all correct. Browser-checked `/add` renders with no console errors after adding the new popup/state.
## 2026-07-16 — Remove the XP feature entirely - **Why:** user reported still seeing "+0 XP" on the `/add` result screen after a link ingest and asked to remove XP from the whole website, not just zero it out. XP had already been half-retired on 2026-07-13 (dashboard progress bars/stars hidden, `awardXp()` always wrote `xp:0` to `profiles`), but the plumbing — DB column, response fields, computed-then-discarded values, the leftover "+{xp} XP" line on `/add` — was all still live. - **Removed everywhere:** `xpForReview()` deleted from `src/lib/srs.ts`; `awardXp()` renamed to `advanceStreak()` in `src/lib/progress.ts` (dropped the `xp` param/column entirely, keeps only the actual streak-advance logic it always really did); `xp` field dropped from `Profile` and `ReportCard` in `src/lib/types.ts`, from `IngestResult` in `src/lib/data.ts`, and from `demoProfile` in `src/lib/demo.ts`; every `xp: 0` / `xp: reports.reduce(...)` response field removed from `/api/ingest`, `/api/quiz`, and the three demo-mode report-builders in `data.ts` (including the now-dead `newTopics` counter in `route.ts` that existed only to feed the old XP formula); the visible `+{result.xp} XP` line removed from `src/app/add/page.tsx`. `profiles.xp` column dropped from `supabase/schema.sql` (the fresh-install reference) — **not applied to the live Supabase DB** (no migration tooling/connection available here; harmless to leave an unused always-0 column there, user can `ALTER TABLE profiles DROP COLUMN xp;` themselves if they want it gone live too). - **Verified:** repo-wide grep for `xp`/`awardXp`/`xpForReview` across `src/` and `supabase/` returns nothing; `tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` clean; browser-checked in demo mode — dashboard streak ("6 day streak") still renders correctly, `/add` page and its guest demo-gate popup still work, no console errors. The actual `/add` "done" result screen (where `+0 XP` lived) still isn't reachable in demo mode by design (guests are gated before ever calling `/api/ingest`) — same live-verification limitation noted in the 2026-07-16 guardrails entry below.
## 2026-07-16 — Fix false-positive gibberish rejection on real link ingests - **Why:** user reported `https://arpitbhayani.me/blogs/bm25` (a real, substantial technical blog post) got rejected by `/api/ingest` with "That doesn't look like real content to learn from" — the gibberish guardrail added earlier today (see the entry below) was too aggressive. - **Root cause 1:** the repeated-pattern-flood regex in `looksLikeGibberish()` (`src/lib/guardrails.ts`) matched ANY 10+ char run of a repeated 1-4-char unit, including pure punctuation. The BM25 post renders its formulas as ASCII fraction bars (`──────────────────────────────────────────────`), which tripped it immediately — any technical writing with ASCII dividers/table borders/box-drawing rules would fail the same way. **Fix:** the flood check now only fires when the repeated unit contains a letter or digit; punctuation-only floods that really are spam (e.g. `>>> >>> >>>` quote-chain garbage) are still caught by the word-shape check below (none of their "words" contain a letter). - **Root cause 2 (more severe, would have rejected almost any real article):** the distinct-character-ratio check assumed real prose has "real variety" in its character distribution — but English only has 36 possible alnum characters (26 letters + 10 digits), so that ratio mathematically saturates and collapses toward 0 for ANY text past roughly 300 characters (~2 sentences), independent of repetition. Verified against unrelated control paragraphs (a Wikipedia-style paragraph on the Roman Empire, a photosynthesis paragraph) — both tripped it too. Its intended purpose (catching short repeating alphabets) was already redundantly covered by the flood-regex and the distinct-word-ratio check, so it was **removed entirely** rather than re-tuned. - **Verified:** replayed the actual `fetchReadable()` HTML→text extraction against the real page (curled it, ran the exact route.ts regex pipeline in a scratch script) to reproduce the bug outside the app, then ran the real `src/lib/guardrails.ts` (via `tsx`, not a reimplementation) against a 12-case suite: all original spam/mash cases (keyboard mash, `hahaha`, `asdasd`, quote-flood, symbol spam, repeated-word spam) still correctly rejected; the real BM25 blog, short/medium/long real prose, a markdown-divider+prose mix, and a JS snippet all correctly pass now. `tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build` clean.
## 2026-07-16 — Team architecture doc (backend/AI/RAG/auth/security) - **Why:** user asked for a printable, detailed, diagram-heavy reference for the team covering backend, AI, RAG, auth and security (frontend deliberately excluded) — specifically clarifying where vector storage/search runs (Supabase Postgres+pgvector, as SQL functions) vs where embedding/generation calls originate (Vercel serverless → Gemini API). - **New `ARCHITECTURE.md`** at repo root: HLD (system context + deployment topology Mermaid diagrams), full ER diagram of every table (core schema + RAG v1/v2), auth/proxy sequence diagram, security threat→mitigation table, AI-call-budget table, RAG pipeline diagram with an explicit Vercel-vs-Supabase-vs-Gemini compute split, ingest/quiz/reindex sequence diagrams, file map, and a backlog/known-gaps table pulled from TASKS.md. Sourced directly from the schema files and `gemini.ts`/`rag.ts`/`route.ts`/`proxy.ts`/`guardrails.ts`, not just PROJECT_SUMMARY.md, to keep it accurate at the code level. - **Not wired into the session-context-protocol trio** (PROJECT_SUMMARY/SESSION_LOG/TASKS) as a fourth required read — it's a standalone team-facing deliverable, regenerate manually after auth/RAG/AI-budget changes.
## 2026-07-16 — Ingest guardrails: gibberish rejection, prompt-injection hardening, output validation, link-scheme XSS fix - **Why:** user asked for guardrails on ingest text/URL input against gibberish and injection attacks. Audited the real attack surface first: `grep -r dangerouslySetInnerHTML` returns nothing anywhere in the app, so AI-extracted text (topic summary/key_points/questions/facts) can only ever render as inert text via React's default escaping — EXCEPT `src/components/Markdown.tsx`'s link rule, which built `href={m[2]}` straight from parsed `[text](url)` with no scheme check (a real `javascript:`/`data:` URI XSS gap, though only reachable today via the personal-notes feature, not AI content). - **New `src/lib/guardrails.ts`** (dependency-free, matches `chunk.ts`'s style): `looksLikeGibberish()` — repeated-pattern-run regex, meaningful-char ratio (catches pure symbol spam, which dodges the other two checks), distinct-char ratio, distinct-word/word-shape ratio. Deliberately lenient; verified against a 10-case suite (keyboard mash / repeated char / repeated word / symbol spam all correctly rejected; real prose, LaTeX-heavy notes, an email chain, and a JS code snippet all correctly pass). `sanitizeField()` strips control characters (built from numeric char codes, not literal escapes, after literal `\u00XX` sequences kept getting mangled in transit while authoring this) and clamps length. - **Wired into `/api/ingest`:** gibberish check runs right after the length/truncation check, before the duplicate-hash lookup or any Gemini call — applies uniformly to pasted text AND link-fetched text (both converge on the same `text` variable). - **Every field `extractKnowledge()` returns is now validated/clamped before touching the DB**: topic `category` checked against `CATEGORY_COLORS` keys (`src/lib/types.ts`) with a `"General"` fallback — previously never validated; title/summary/key_points/connection-reason/question-prompt/model_answer/options/fact all run through `sanitizeField()` with per-field length caps. Defense in depth against a manipulated or hallucinating model — JSON mode bounds the *shape*, not what's inside the strings. - **Prompt-injection hardening** in `extractKnowledge()` (`src/lib/gemini.ts`): the pasted/fetched text and the RAG grounding block are now explicitly framed as "untrusted data, not instructions," with an explicit rule to never follow embedded commands — relevant because link ingestion feeds arbitrary third-party webpage text straight into the prompt. - **Fixed the `Markdown.tsx` href gap:** new `isSafeHref()` only renders `[text](url)` as a clickable `<a>` for `http`/`https`/`mailto` (or relative) schemes; anything else (`javascript:`, `data:`) now renders as inert plain text. - **Verified:** `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean. Gibberish heuristic runtime-tested via `tsx` against the 10-case suite (all pass). Markdown fix verified LIVE in the browser (demo mode, `/notes` is localStorage-only so this one *is* exercisable without a real backend): typed a note body containing `[danger](javascript:alert(document.cookie))`, `[data uri](data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>)` and `[safe link](https://example.com)`, switched to Read view, inspected the actual rendered DOM — the safe link became a real `<a href="https://example.com">`, the other two rendered as plain non-clickable text. Could not exercise the ingest-route changes live (no real Supabase locally, demo mode never calls `/api/ingest`).
## 2026-07-15 — Fix silent link-ingest failure, URL-based fast-path dedup - **Why:** user reported "add via link shows loading then does nothing." Root cause found in `src/lib/data.ts`'s `api()` helper: `throw new Error(err.error ?? "Request failed")` — `??` only falls back on null/undefined, not `""`. Over HTTP/2 (Vercel prod), a failed fetch's `res.statusText` is ALWAYS `""` (no reason phrases), so any non-JSON error response (platform crash/timeout) produced `new Error("")`; the `/add` page's `{error && <p>{error}</p>}` then rendered nothing since `""` is falsy — spinner reverts silently, no visible error. Likely trigger: link ingestion is the slow path (page fetch + Gemini + several writes), and the prior RAG-hardening session added ~6-8 more sequential DB round-trips to that same request, raising timeout risk. - **Fixed** `api()` to use `||` and include `res.status` in the fallback so an error is never blank. - **Added a URL-based fast-path duplicate check** in `/api/ingest`: checks `entries.source_url` for this user BEFORE fetching the page, so resubmitting an already-ingested link now skips the network fetch AND the Gemini call entirely (near-instant response) rather than re-running the full slow path — this also catches "same link" duplicates the content-hash check could miss (re-fetched pages can differ slightly — ads/timestamps/related-post widgets — even for the same URL). - **Extracted `duplicateResponse()`** (shared by both the new URL check and the existing content-hash check from the prior session) and wrapped it in its own try/catch so a lookup hiccup building the "already added" response can never crash the request. - **Root cause CONFIRMED** via the user's Network tab: `/api/ingest` returned Vercel's own `FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT` (504) — the function genuinely ran out of time, not a code exception. Fixed for real (not just surfaced) two ways in `src/app/api/ingest/route.ts`: - **Deferred all non-critical RAG bookkeeping to Next's `after()`**: `indexContent()` (chunk+embed+registry writes for the new entry) and `linkRetrievalLogToEntry()` now run AFTER the response is sent instead of blocking it — both are strictly best-effort and their results were never used in the response. This was the biggest avoidable contributor (embedding calls + several extra DB round-trips added by the prior RAG-hardening session, all on the request's critical path). - **`fetchReadable()`'s redirect-following now shares one 20s deadline across all hops** instead of a fresh 15s timeout per hop (previously up to 5×15s=75s worst case for a redirect-heavy link — enough to blow the function's duration on its own regardless of anything else). - **Verified:** `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` clean, `after()` resolves correctly on Next 16.2.10. Still could not reproduce live (no real Supabase locally, demo mode never calls the real route) — asked the user to retry against production.
## 2026-07-15 — RAG pipeline hardening: document registry, hybrid search, observability - **Why:** user asked whether the RAG pipeline actually implements three pillars they named (indexing pipeline w/ registry+hash+delete+versioned deployment; hybrid retrieval+reranking; observability). Audit against actual source found only the basic chunk/embed/store + vector-kNN halves existed — no dedup, no delete/replace semantics, no index versioning, no keyword search, no reranking, no observability at all. Confirmed a real latent bug in the process: re-ingesting identical text/URL silently created duplicate `entries`/`knowledge_chunks`/`questions` forever, burning a Gemini call + daily-cap slot each time. - **User chose to skip cross-encoder reranking** after being offered three options (local transformers.js / Gemini LLM reranking / hybrid-only) — no ML runtime exists in this stack and an LLM reranker would break the "exactly 1 Gemini call per ingest" budget. Ships as hybrid vector+keyword only; reranking is a documented TASKS backlog item, not silently dropped. - **New `supabase/schema-rag-v2.sql`** (requires `schema-rag.sql` already applied): `rag_documents` registry (content-hash dedup, partial unique index scoped to `source='entry'`), `knowledge_chunks` alters (`document_id`, `index_version`, generated `content_tsv`+GIN), per-user `rag_index_pointers` (the version "alias"), `rag_retrieval_logs` (chunk-level attribution), version-aware `match_knowledge_chunks`, new `match_knowledge_chunks_hybrid` (RRF between vector kNN and `ts_rank_cd` keyword search — lexemes OR'd, not AND'd, since the real query is a ~2000-char excerpt not a search phrase), new `rag_index_version_correlation()`. - **`src/lib/rag.ts` rewritten**: `hashContent()`, `findDuplicateEntry()` (checked in `/api/ingest` BEFORE the Gemini call — a duplicate resubmission now reuses the existing entry with a friendly message instead of reprocessing), `indexContent()`/`indexAnswers()` made idempotent via a private `upsertDocument()` (re-indexing an entry deletes-then-replaces its chunks, never duplicates), `getActiveIndexVersion`/`setActiveIndexVersion`/`pruneIndexVersion`, `reindexDocument()`, `retrieveContext()` now hybrid-first with fallback to plain vector search + returns a log id, `linkRetrievalLogToEntry()` (retrieval runs before the entry exists, so the log backfills `entry_id` after), `getRetrievalMetrics()`, `getIndexVersionCorrelation()` (explicitly documented as correlation, not causal — retrieval quality is several steps removed from a quiz score). - **New `POST /api/rag/reindex`**: self-serve, RLS-only (no service-role key), paginated (200 docs/call, safe to call repeatedly). Only flips the version pointer + prunes the retired version once a full pass has zero pending documents — a naive per-call swap would delete not-yet-processed documents' only copy. Nothing calls this automatically; it's infrastructure for a future chunk-size/embedding-model change. - **`/add` duplicate UX**: `IngestResult` gained `duplicate?`/`message?`; the result card shows "You already added '…'" + the reuse message and hides the now-misleading `+0 XP` line when a duplicate is detected. - **Verified**: `npx tsc --noEmit` clean, `npm run build` clean. Cannot exercise the new SQL live — no Supabase CLI/local backing DB in this environment (same limitation the original RAG layer had). New SQL was code-reviewed for RLS coverage, cascade correctness (two independent FK paths into `knowledge_chunks` converge correctly), and RRF query logic. **Flagged to user**: TASKS.md's "run schema-rag.sql" item has been unchecked since RAG was first added 2026-07-15 — worth confirming whether the original RAG layer was ever actually activated in production.
## 2026-07-15 — Rebrand: Engramia → Knovis - **Why:** user-requested rename. "Knovis" = knowledge + vision, and deliberately echoes "novice" — every true learner stays one. - **Every user-facing mention updated:** `Nav.tsx` wordmark + tagline ("kno·vis — knowledge meets vision"), `Footer.tsx` brand/tagline/copyright, `login/page.tsx` welcome heading + tagline paragraph, `layout.tsx` metadata title/description, `profile/page.tsx` display-name helper text, `tour.ts` step copy ("Welcome to Knovis" / "That's Knovis" / plan-step body). - **About page origin story rewritten**, not just find-replaced: the old paragraph defining "engram" (physical trace a memory leaves) is replaced with the actual Knovis naming story — landing on "knowledge + vision," noticing it sounds like "novice," and leaning into that on purpose ("every real learner stays a novice forever — you just get better at revisiting"). Title/description metadata and the closing "because of Knovis" line updated too. - **Internal identifiers renamed for full consistency** (deliberate, not just cosmetic): guest cookie `engramia_guest` → `knovis_guest` (`src/proxy.ts` — hardcoded literal, kept in lockstep with `GUEST_COOKIE` in `src/lib/demo.ts`, since edge middleware can't share the client-only constant); `EngramiaBot` → `KnovisBot` User-Agent in the link-ingest fetch (`api/ingest/route.ts`); every `engramia.*` localStorage/sessionStorage key → `knovis.*` (`data.ts` demo-state, `notes.ts` notes/seeded/event keys, `tour.ts` seen/state keys); `package.json` name field. - **Consequence (accepted, not a bug):** pre-existing guest cookies and localStorage from the Engramia era simply stop matching the new key names and reset silently — no migration code, no errors, matches how demo/guest state already behaves (best-effort, never persisted server-side for guests). - **Docs synced:** `README.md` title, `PROJECT_SUMMARY.md` title + Branding section + every stale `engramia_guest`/`engramia.*` reference elsewhere in the file. - **Verified:** `grep -ri engram src package.json README.md` returns nothing; tsc clean.
## 2026-07-15 — Tour interactivity, demo add-gate popup, review-demo step - **Why:** follow-up feedback on the guided tour — spotlighted features (calendar, brain) were inert behind the tour's click shield; the tour had no Review step at all; and guests hitting "Extract & save" got a fake simulated success instead of an honest gate. - **Spotlighted features are now genuinely clickable during the tour.** Root cause: the full-screen overlay div always had `pointer-events:auto` (default), silently swallowing every click even over the "see-through" spotlight window. Fixed in `src/components/TutorialTour.tsx`: the overlay wrapper is `pointer-events:none` whenever a spotlight exists (only `.tour-card` opts back into `pointer-events:auto`, added to `.tour-card` in globals.css), so clicks fall straight through to the real page underneath. Verified: typing in the `/brain` search box, clicking a search result to open the topic info card, and clicking a lit calendar day to open its real day-report modal — all now work mid-tour. - **`ProgressCalendar`'s day-report modal bumped `z-50` → `z-[125]`** (`src/components/ProgressCalendar.tsx`) so it renders above the tour's `z-120` dim layer instead of being invisible behind it when opened during the "Study calendar" step. - **Link navigation blocked globally while the tour is active**, not just visually implied: a capture-phase `document` click listener in TutorialTour intercepts any click on an `<a>` (Nav, plan rows, notes, blog cards, "Read the full topic", …) and prevents it, so a stray click can't route the visitor away from the guided flow — non-anchor interactions are unaffected. Escape hatch: a `data-allow-nav` ancestor lets specific links through (used below). - **New "Try a review" tour step** (`custom: "review-demo"` in `src/lib/tour.ts`, inserted right after "One-tap review" — now 14 steps total): a fully self-contained mock quiz (`DEMO_REVIEW_QUESTION`, a made-up spaced-repetition question) rendered inside the tour card itself via a new `ReviewDemoCard` component — never touches real quiz state, Supabase, or demo progress. Pick an option → Submit answer → mocked report card (score ring reusing `BigScoreRing` from `ReportCardView.tsx`, feedback text, correct answer) → Continue advances the tour like any other step. - **Demo-mode "Add" is now an honest gate, not a fake success:** `src/app/add/page.tsx` — submitting while `isDemo` no longer calls the simulated `ingestText`/`ingestLink` demo branches; instead it shows a translucent `.overlay-panel` popup ("This is a demo — guest sessions can explore freely but can't add new data") with "Sign in" (marked `data-allow-nav` so it still navigates even mid-tour) and "Not now". Removed the old "extraction is simulated" helper copy accordingly. - **Verified in browser:** confirmed via DOM/console-level checks (the browser-pane renderer was flaky again this session — screenshots intermittently blank/stuck, and the `navigate` tool itself was observed to zero out `window.innerWidth/innerHeight` on some tabs; worked around with fresh tabs + `location.href` navigation instead). Add-page popup appears on submit in demo mode; calendar day click opens the real report-card modal (`z-[125]` div found with full report text) mid-tour; `/brain` search input accepts typing and a search-result click opens the real topic info card mid-tour, while its "Read the full topic" link click does NOT navigate (still on `/brain`); the review-demo step renders the mock question, Submit reveals the mock report card, Continue advances to "Momentum". tsc clean.
## 2026-07-15 — Guided feature tour (first visit + every demo entry) - **Why:** review feedback — the site is feature-rich but had no onboarding; new visitors need to be shown what everything does. - **13-step spotlight tour** matching the design language: dim overlay, glowing gold spotlight cut-out around each feature (giant box-shadow technique), translucent blurred step card (`.tour-card` in globals.css — Space Grotesk titles, JetBrains-mono "tour · N / 13" micro label, progress dots, gradient Next pill, Back/skip, Esc/arrow keys). Steps: welcome → nav → revision plan → Start/review CTA → momentum/streak → calendar → fact of the day → personal notes → /brain 3D canvas (full-viewport spotlight, scene stays visible) → brain search → /blogs library → /add ingest form → closing card (copy varies by mode). - **Wiring:** `src/lib/tour.ts` (step script + storage: seen flag `engramia.tour.seen.v1` in localStorage, active state `engramia.tour.state.v1` in sessionStorage so it survives guest-mode hard navigations) + `src/components/TutorialTour.tsx` mounted once in `layout.tsx`. Cross-page steps `router.push` themselves; anchors are `data-tour` attributes (Nav ×2 desktop/mobile — visible one wins, dashboard ×6, brain ×2, blogs, add); 150 ms poll handles late client-side data, centered fallback if an anchor never appears. - **Flows (as requested):** first browser visit → /login auto-arms mode "first" + guest cookie (demo data powers the tour) → tour runs → complete/skip → guest cookie cleared → back to /login. Every "Continue as guest" click arms mode "demo" → tour replays every time → finishing stays in the demo. TutorialTour also self-arms once for unseen visitors already in demo mode (e.g. abandoned mid-tour in another tab). - **Verified in browser (demo):** full first-visit run → post-tour state correct (seen=1, state cleared, guest cookie cleared, login form renders); "Continue as guest" → tour restarts at 1/13; stepped through all 13 incl. cross-page /brain → /blogs → /add navigations, spotlight tracking and card placement; demo finale shows "Start exploring →" and closes into the demo dashboard; no console errors; tsc clean. NB: browser-pane renderer flaky again (offset screenshots / stuck clicks) — ref-clicks + DOM-level JS checks used.
## 2026-07-15 — Attempted-tracking bug fix, security review, Gemini fallback, RAG pipeline, DB decision - **"Answered questions show as not attempted" + reroute bug FIXED.** Two root causes: (1) UTC-vs-local day mismatch — `/api/plan` done-tracking, the calendar and the heatmap all bucketed by `created_at.slice(0,10)` (UTC), so an evening-IST review landed on the next UTC day and never marked its plan item done. New `src/lib/dates.ts` centralises day math around the USER'S LOCAL day; client passes tz offset to `/api/plan?tz=`, quiz `finish`/plan `complete`; all client bucketing uses `localDayKey`. (2) The review page read `?topic=` once via `window.location.search` on mount, so clicking a different plan row (same `/review` pathname) never re-ran init → stale session reopened the old question. Now uses `useSearchParams` (Suspense-wrapped) with a full state reset per `topic` change. Also: `doneTopicsToday()` now unions `reviews` rows AND `topics.last_reviewed_at` (safety net if a reviews insert fails); full-plan runs sort pending-first (never open on an "already reviewed" card); a last-pending single-task deep link now closes the day itself; streak advances on every attempted session (was gated behind XP, which is retired → 0). - **Demo progress persists** to `localStorage` (`engramia.demo.v1`) — guest reviews/reports/streak survive reload/hard-nav; `done`/`completed` reset on a new local day like the real backend. - **Security review (whole codebase).** Added an **SSRF guard** to link ingest (`assertPublicHttpUrl`: http(s)+standard ports only; blocks loopback/private/link-local/CGNAT IPv4, IPv6 ULA/link-local, `localhost`/`.local`/`.internal`, creds-in-URL; redirects followed MANUALLY 5-hop so each hop is re-checked). **Per-user daily ingest cap** (`INGEST_DAILY_LIMIT`, default 20) + 120k-char store cap. All API routes now guard `request.json()` (malformed→400); `/auth/callback` redirects to `/login?error=auth` on session-exchange failure. Confirmed RLS covers every table incl. new `knowledge_chunks`. No XSS sinks, no committed secrets (`.env.local` gitignored). - **Gemini model fallback** (`gemini.ts`): 429/quota or 503/overload retries down a chain (`GEMINI_MODEL` → `GEMINI_MODEL_FALLBACKS`, default flash → flash-lite → 2.0-flash → 2.0-flash-lite); a failed model cools down 5 min per warm instance. - **RAG pipeline** (source-of-truth grounding). New `supabase/schema-rag.sql` (pgvector, `knowledge_chunks`, HNSW cosine index, `match_knowledge_chunks` RPC, RLS). `chunk.ts` (overlapping ~350-token chunker), `embedTexts()` in gemini.ts (`gemini-embedding-001`, 768-dim, L2-normalised, doc/query task types), `rag.ts` (`indexContent`/`indexAnswers`/`retrieveContext`/`formatContext`). Ingest retrieves the user's prior related chunks → grounds `extractKnowledge`, then indexes the new entry; quiz finish indexes typed answers. Everything best-effort: dormant + no errors until the SQL is run. Blog long-form-from-RAG deliberately deferred (would add an AI call per page view). - **DB decision: STAY on Supabase, don't migrate to TiDB.** TiDB Cloud gives 5GB (vs 500MB) but has NO auth and no Postgres RLS — both load-bearing here (browser-direct queries rely on RLS; whole app is `@supabase/ssr`+auth). Migration = rebuild auth + hand-roll per-user isolation + full API-route rewrite + pgvector→TiDB-vector port. 500MB is workable for ~50 users at 768-dim; the real free-tier gotcha is the 7-day inactivity pause. Path: upgrade to Supabase Pro ($25/mo → 8GB, no pause, backups) when needed — far cheaper than migrating. - **Verified:** tsc + prod build clean (16 routes; `/review` still prerenders static behind Suspense). In browser (demo): single-task deep link → answer → 80% report card; nav Review → full 10-item plan pending-first; answer d1 → hard reload → "9 remaining" + streak persisted + d1 done; click d3 → d3's question (no reroute to d1); no console errors. RAG untestable in demo (server/real-mode only) but build+typecheck green and graceful-degradation verified by design. graphify updated (461 nodes).
## 2026-07-14 — Quiz-kind overhaul, day-report calendar, blog Q&A, streak bar, footer + About - **Flashcards fully removed** (extraction, ingest save, `rate` API action, `getFlashcards`/`rateFlashcard`, demo data, review "cards" phase, `Flashcard` type). Question bank grew instead: kinds now open / quickfire / mcq / **truefalse** (options ["True","False"] + correct_index) / **multi** (multi-correct, `correct_indices` jsonb column added to `questions` — both schema files updated with `add column if not exists`). Ingest asks Gemini for 7-8 questions/topic; quiz `start` rotates all five kinds; multi picks JSON-serialised into `quiz_answers.answer` (no answers-table change); `finish` grades truefalse like mcq and multi with partial credit (exact=5 else 0-4 by hits−wrong). - **Report card shows EVERY question** with full Q&A (prompt, your answer, correct answer, feedback, option highlighting incl. "your pick"), not counts. New shared `src/components/ReportCardView.tsx` renders it (used by /review + calendar modal). `getLatestReportToday()` now merges ALL of today's graded sessions via new `getDayReport()`. - **Calendar day → day report:** lit days on `ProgressCalendar` are buttons; clicking opens a modal with that day's merged report card (`getDayReport(day)`: merges `quiz_sessions` reports of the day, falls back to rebuilding from `reviews` rows for older history). Demo keeps graded reports in `demoState.reports`. - **Blog Q&A:** `/blogs/[id]` gained a "Questions & answers" section — every bank question for the topic as collapsible `<details>` with kind chip, options (correct highlighted) and model answer (`getTopicQuestions()`; questions belong to exactly one topic so each lives on its most meaningful blog by construction). - **Streak milestone bar** restored on the Momentum card: ladder 3/7/14/…/365, "N days to a M-day streak", record-aware motivational line (replaces the removed mastery bar). - **Branding:** meaning tagline "en·gram — the trace a memory leaves in your brain" under the Nav wordmark; login subtitle explains the engram; layout metadata description rewritten. Verified no stray "Engram" naming remains. - **Footer + About:** global `Footer.tsx` in layout (brand + meaning, Explore links, Connect: About us / Contact us mailto / LinkedIn). New quirky `/about` page about Sanyam Gupta (origin story, "things I firmly believe", contact CTAs — email + LinkedIn). - **Verified in browser (demo):** 10-question session mixing all five kinds (correct/incorrect/partial/skip paths) → report card 76% with all 10 items fully detailed; all-skip session → report with 10 skipped items; "See report" → merged day report; calendar day click → 73% day modal with 6 Q&A cards; blog d1 shows 3 Q&A with reveal; About + footer render. tsc + prod build clean; no console errors. NB: browser pane renderer got flaky (black screenshots / scroll timeouts) — DOM-level JS checks used, as in prior sessions. graphify updated (425 nodes).
## 2026-07-13 — Rebranding to Engramia + Mastery/XP removal + True/False Statements + Detailed completed reviews - **Rebranding to Engramia:** Renamed project "Engram" to "Engramia" globally across code and configurations (titles, cookies, localStorage keys, packages, etc.). - **Mastery and XP Metric Removal:** Disabled and hidden all XP and level components (XP totals, progress bars, level progress stars, and constellation paths). Sizing of brain graph nodes now leverages topic `review_count`. Dashboard "At a glance" stats simplified to "Total topics" and "Tasks today" only. Daily plans now rank/sort topics by `review_count` instead of `mastery`. - **See report Button:** When the daily revision plan is completed, the homepage "Start" button dynamically changes to "See report" which navigates to `/review` and pulls up the latest graded quiz report from today immediately. - **True or False Statements:** Flashcards have been replaced with True/False statements. Gemini ingest updated to generate statements under `flashcards` with exact `"True"` or `"False"` answers. Users click "True" or "False" buttons and are immediately graded (automatically rating spacing interval based on correctness). - **Detailed Done Review History:** Already reviewed topics now show a detailed card containing the question prompt, what you answered, the correct stored answer, and grading feedback. Guest attempts are saved in local memory so they populate the activity heatmap calendar and the review breakdown details.
## 2026-07-13 — Review bug fixes + /blogs page - **Bug 1 fixed — already-answered questions re-opening:** `/review` now loads ALL plan items (not filtered to remaining-only). Items with `it.done=true` show an "Already reviewed today" card (✓ icon, topic name, "locked in for today") with a "Next topic →" button instead of re-asking the question. The `loadItem()` callback short-circuits to `setPhase("already-done")` when `it.done` is true. - **Bug 2 fixed — report card after 1 question:** Root cause: old code filtered the plan to only `remaining` items — if 1 item remained, 1 question then report. Now the full plan is passed (all items); done items auto-skip via the `already-done` phase; quiz session is started only for `pendingIds` (non-done topics). After all items are stepped through, finalize() runs. - **Bug 3 fixed — plan/stats count discrepancy:** Removed the `dueCount` ("Due today") stat (topics where `next_review_at <= now` — different from plan count). Replaced with **"In plan"** stat that reads `plan.items.length`. Also added a subtitle to the "Revision plan" heading: `· N topics, M remaining` so the full count is always visible even when collapsed. - **Bug 4 fixed — /blogs page + nav:** Created `src/app/blogs/page.tsx` — standalone library page listing all topic blogs as filterable cards. **Moved** `src/app/brain/[id]` to `src/app/blogs/[id]` to completely decouple the blog read from the brain route. Added **Blogs** link to `Nav.tsx` (BookIcon, between Brain and Add). `blogs/[id]` blog page: breadcrumb → "← All blogs" → `/blogs`; bottom CTAs: "All blogs" + "Brain graph". Dashboard links to blogs via `/blogs/${t.id}`. - **Stats fix (At a glance):** Clarified the dashboard stats row by wrapping it in a section with a "At a glance" heading, and renamed the labels from "Topics", "In plan", "Mastery" to "Total topics", "Tasks today", and "Avg. mastery" to remove ambiguity.
## 2026-07-13 — Guest mode fix, plan done-tracking, review error card, blog pills - **Guest/demo on production FIXED:** "Continue as guest" was a plain `Link href="/"` → proxy bounced unauthenticated visitors straight back to /login (the reload loop). Now: button sets `engram_guest=1` cookie (`enableGuestMode()` in demo.ts) + hard navigation; `src/proxy.ts` lets guests through and **deletes the cookie whenever a real session exists**; `isDemo` = no Supabase env OR guest cookie. Sign-in now hard-navigates (`window.location.assign`) so stale module-level `isDemo` re-evaluates. Render-time isDemo uses (Nav badge/auth controls, /add notice, /profile notice) moved to post-mount state to avoid SSR hydration mismatches (cookie invisible to server). - **Plan done-tracking:** `PlanItem.done` computed at read time — real mode from today's `reviews` rows (`doneTopicsToday()` in /api/plan, applied to saved AND fresh plans), demo from `demoState.done`. Dashboard: **green dot** (#43d6b5) for remaining tasks, done rows dimmed + ✓ + strikethrough + "Completed today" and **sorted last**; section shows 5 rows with **"Show all N tasks (M remaining)"** toggle. `MAX_ITEMS` 6→10; demo plan padded to 10 items. Full `/review` run now covers only remaining items (replays all when everything's done); deep-linked single tasks unchanged. - **Review empty-page FIXED:** when a question couldn't load, phase was "question" with `question=null` → nothing rendered. Now a proper error card ("Couldn't load this question" + Skip topic / Try again) shows the real cause; `startQuiz` failures are captured into `startError`; `/api/quiz start` returns an explicit "run the latest supabase/schema.sql" message when the quiz tables are missing (42P01) — the likely production cause. - **Dashboard knowledge-graph card:** topic pills now link to `/brain/<id>` blog pages (card restructured: header links to /brain, pills are individual Links). - **Verified (demo):** guest button → cookie + dashboard; 10-task plan collapsed to 5 + toggle; completed d13 → green dots on the rest, d13 dimmed/✓/"Completed today"/last, "9 remaining", full run "1 of 9"; pills href /brain/d1…; no console errors; tsc + prod build clean. NB: browser tab renderer got flaky mid-session (black screenshots/scroll timeouts) — DOM-level checks used for the tail end.
## 2026-07-13 — AI-call minimisation redesign: question bank, batch report card, MCQs, fact of the day - **Goal:** minimum LLM calls. Now exactly 1 Gemini call per ingest + 1 per finished quiz session; plan narrative & fact of the day are zero-AI. See PROJECT_SUMMARY "AI-call budget". - **Ingest pre-generates everything:** `extractKnowledge()` prompt extended → per topic 5-6 bank questions (open/quickfire/**mcq** with 4 options + correct_index, model_answer, difficulty) + 1-2 facts. New tables `questions`, `facts` (+ `quiz_sessions`, `quiz_answers`) in schema.sql with RLS — **must re-run schema.sql in Supabase**. - **Quiz flow rebuilt** (`/api/quiz`: start/answer/finish/rate): `start` builds a session from the bank (kind rotation, mastery-matched difficulty, least-asked first; local fallback question for pre-bank topics; client never receives answers); each answer **saved to DB on submit**; `finish` = MCQs graded deterministically + ONE `gradeSession()` batch call → **report card** (score ring, XP, summary, strengths/focus chips, per-item feedback + model answers, MCQ option highlighting, skipped items). Heuristic keyword fallback if Gemini fails; idempotent (report stored on session). SRS per topic from session average; reviews rows keep calendar/heatmap alive; flashcards stay self-rated (no AI). - **Review page rewritten:** no per-question grading; "Answers are graded together at the end" bar, MCQ option pills, Save & next/finish, then "Building your report card…" → report. Deep-link `?topic=` still runs 1-question sessions (verified). - **Plan narrative sans Gemini:** `composeNarrative()` — day-rotating headline templates + insight reusing a stored `topic_links.reason` between today's topics. - **Fact of the day:** dashboard card (right column, bulb icon) via `getFactOfTheDay()` — deterministic day-hash pick from `facts` (fallback: topic key_point). Demo pool `demoFacts`. - **Demo mode** mirrors it all: `demoQuestionBank` (incl. MCQs), local grading/report. - **Verified in browser (demo):** full 6-item session (open → flashcards → MCQ → quickfire → flashcards → skip) → report card 80%, +109 XP, 3 answered · 1 skipped, MCQ correct-option highlight, skipped shows model answer; single-task deep link → 1-item report; fact card on dashboard; no console errors. tsc + prod build clean. graphify updated (403 nodes).
## 2026-07-13 — Personal notes feature + blog source attribution - **Personal notes (`/notes`):** new OneNote-style page — left tree of notes + nested **subnotes** (recursive `NoteRow`, expand/collapse), right editor with a **Write/Read** toggle (markdown ↔ rendered), autosave, breadcrumb, add-subnote, delete-subtree. Deliberately **not in the knowledge graph** — stored in **localStorage** (`src/lib/notes.ts`, key `engram.notes.v1`); writes fire `NOTES_EVENT`/`storage` so views stay in sync. One-time seeded welcome note. New `src/components/Markdown.tsx` (dependency-free md renderer) powers the Read view. `Note`/`TopicSource` types added to `types.ts`. - **Dashboard "Personal notes" section:** left column, lists top-level note headers (title · updated · subnote count) linking to `/notes?note=<id>`; empty-state invites the first note. (Access = /notes OR this section, per request.) - **Blog source (task 2):** `/brain/[id]` now shows a "Source" line — the entry's `source_url` as a clickable host+path link, or "Text input" when pasted (never the raw text). `getTopicSource()` in `data.ts` (real: `entry_topics`→`entries`; demo: `demoTopicSource`, d1/d3/d4/d11 = arxiv/github URLs, rest text). - **Verified in browser (demo):** notes page renders, Read mode renders md (bold/lists/code/quote/links), subnote nesting works, autosave persists to localStorage across reload; dashboard section shows the seed note; blog shows arxiv link (d1) and "Text input" (d5). tsc clean, no console errors. - **Auto-push:** committed and pushed to `origin/main` without confirmation, per standing instruction. Graphify graph refresh still **pending** — CLI (`graphifyy`) not yet installed this session.
## 2026-07-13 — Dashboard/brain polish: rich insight, clickable plan, topic blogs, smaller labels - **"Today's connection" → "The thread today"** and rendered rich: new `src/components/RichText.tsx` renders inline markdown (**bold**/*italic*/`code`) as styled spans instead of `stripMarkdown()`, so Gemini emphasis survives on the dashboard insight. - **Clickable revision-plan rows:** each dashboard plan `<li>` is now a `Link` to `/review?topic=<id>` (hover arrow). `src/app/review/page.tsx` reads the param from `window.location.search`; a matching id runs a single-task session. Guarded with `singleTask` so finishing one deep-linked task does NOT call `markPlanCompleted` (won't falsely close the day / bump streak). - **Topic "blog" view:** new route `src/app/brain/[id]/page.tsx` — full read in Fraunces serif (added `--font-serif` in `layout.tsx`; `.article-lead` w/ drop cap + `.article-body` in globals.css), numbered key ideas, connections (link onward), Revise CTA. Brain info card now shows a 2-line-clamped summary + gold **"Read the full topic ↗"** and a clickable title, both opening the blog in a new tab. - **Smaller 3D labels (task 5):** `BrainScene.tsx` node label 0.112→0.086, edge 0.086→0.066 (~23% down). - **graphify CLI:** the real package is `graphifyy` (double-y) via pipx; the npm-global `graphify` is an unrelated pkg. Auto-install was blocked by the sandbox as a typosquat; user is installing it themselves. Graph not refreshed (CLI still unavailable this session). - **Verified:** tsc clean; in browser (demo): plan row → `/review?topic=d10` shows "1 of 1"; `/brain/d1` blog renders (serif, drop cap, 4 key ideas, 3 connections, CTAs); Bayes card shows clamped summary + Read-full link; scene labels visibly smaller. No console errors.
## 2026-07-13 — Vercel Migration & Nav Auth Fix - **Vercel Migration:** Moved deployment from Render to Vercel. Deleted `render.yaml` as it is no longer required. Connected Vercel via Vercel CLI (`npx vercel link` & `npx vercel deploy`). - **Nav Auth UI Fix:** The navigation bar's "Sign in" button was erroneously hidden when `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` was set, even if the user wasn't signed in, due to the `isDemo` check failing. Updated `src/components/Nav.tsx` to maintain a local `authStatus` state using `getProfile()`, explicitly showing the "Sign in" button if the user is unauthenticated, instead of defaulting to "Profile". - **Graphify Note:** Skipped `graphify update .` because the CLI is unavailable in the environment.
## 2026-07-13 — Fix /profile infinite loading (missing profiles row) - **Symptom:** /profile stuck on "Loading…" on the real-Supabase build. Schema *file* was fine — `supabase/schema.sql` already had all profile columns + `handle_new_user` trigger (committed together in `2b3ec32`). Root cause: `getProfile()` did `.single()` and returned `data as Profile` ignoring the error, so a *missing profiles row* (account predates the trigger, or trigger never installed in that project) returned `null` → profile stayed null → hung. Page's `.catch(()=>{})` hid the error. - **Fix:** `getProfile()` (`src/lib/data.ts`) now uses `.maybeSingle()`, throws on real errors, and **self-heals**: if no row, upserts one (`user_metadata.name` → email prefix). `/profile` page now shows an error card (with "run schema.sql" hint) instead of infinite Loading. - **Backfill for existing users:** the trigger only fires on new signups, so pre-existing accounts still need a row (self-heal handles it on next load, or run the SQL insert-from-auth.users backfill). - **Verified:** tsc clean; /profile still renders in demo mode (Demo Learner / LV 5, no regression). Real-Supabase self-heal not exercisable locally (env is demo-only).
## 2026-07-13 — Plan markdown fix, progress calendar, profile page - **Markdown leak:** "Today's connection" showed raw `**…**` from Gemini. Fixed three ways: `writePlanNarrative` prompt now forbids markdown/labels (`src/lib/gemini.ts`); new `stripMarkdown()` in `src/lib/text.ts` applied in `/api/plan` before caching AND at render time in `src/app/page.tsx` (covers already-cached plans in `daily_plans`). - **Progress calendar:** new `src/components/ProgressCalendar.tsx` on the dashboard right column — month view, days lit by review count (3 intensity tiers), today ring, ‹ › month nav (≤11 months back). - **Profile & greeting:** signup form now asks for a name (`options.data.name` → existing `handle_new_user` trigger fills `profiles.display_name`); new `/profile` page (edit display name via new `updateProfile()` + `getUserEmail()` in `src/lib/data.ts`, journey stats, sign out); Nav sign-out button replaced by Profile pill (desktop) and 5th mobile tab. Greeting falls back to "there" only when name truly empty. - **Verified:** tsc clean; in browser (demo mode): calendar renders + month nav works, signup shows name field, profile rename → dashboard greets "Good afternoon, Sanyam". Note: demo-mode name edits reset on full page reload (module state); real users persist via Supabase.
## 2026-07-13 — Brain label overhaul (clickable, Space Grotesk, declutter) - **Problem:** topic name sprites overlapped each other when expanding a topic (all edge labels parked at the same curve fraction; no collision handling), hurting readability and aesthetics. - **Fix (all in `src/components/BrainScene.tsx`):** - Per-frame screen-space declutter: project visible labels → place by priority (hovered > selected > route > edge) → overlap tries vertical nudge via `sprite.center` → else smooth fade-out. - Edge labels staggered along their curves (0.30/0.43/0.56 + alternating lift). - Camera yaw bias (+0.32 landscape) so selection doesn't slide under the info card. - Labels are now **clickable** (node names select; edge names walk to that topic). Raycast checks labels before glows; only when `opacity > 0.3`. - Restyled: Space Grotesk (resolved from `--font-grotesk` at runtime, redrawn on webfont load), smaller (node 0.112 / edge 0.086), hard black outline removed → soft shadow. - **Verified:** tsc clean; live click-through in browser (Bayes' Theorem → clicked edge label → walked to Spaced Repetition).
## Earlier (from git history) - `f7b824f` graphify graph update (proxy migration + login UI) - `24d2c32` middleware.ts → proxy.ts (Next 16 convention) - `310c0a4` removed Google OAuth from login - `78ff2ce` tailwind/postcss → dependencies (prod builds) - `bdb20c5` render.yaml for Render deployment

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
# Tasks
> One active milestone at a time. Move finished items to `SESSION_LOG.md`.
## Active milestone Redesign pass (4 parts, in order): 1. homepage story real estate ✅ → 2. rename Review→Recall ✅ → 3. content audit + visualizations (DSA/SQL/Macro) ✅ → 4. Paper Mode redesign. - [x] **1. Homepage story real estate** — flattened Today's plan back to one list (per-row series colour dot instead of per-story sections), capped `StoryJourney` to top 3 stories (due-today first, then most-recent) + dropped its chapter bead trail, added "All stories (N) →" link to `/blogs`; `/blogs`'s "Read as one story" module now floats started series to the top. (2026-07-20, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **2. Rename "Review" → "Recall"** — route `/review` → `/recall` (+ permanent redirect), nav pill, dashboard/footer/tour copy. Kept `reviews` DB table / SM-2 "review" terminology unchanged. (2026-07-20, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **3. Story content: accuracy pass + visualizations — COMPLETE.** All 34 chapter files (18 DSA, 12 SQL, 6 Macro incl. pilots) now have `viz:*` diagrams (91 total) and were read end-to-end for accuracy (8 real errors found and fixed). Competition Act stays paused from `/blogs` discovery. `npm run build` (331+ pages) + `tsc --noEmit` + the full `viz:*` payload validator all clean. Rolled out via 18 parallel background agents across 3 launch batches (2 interrupted by account session-limits mid-batch — each time verified zero corruption via `git status`/`tsc`/validator before resuming). Full per-file breakdown + accuracy-fix list in SESSION_LOG. `VIZ_ROLLOUT_CHECKLIST.md` (the resume-tracking doc used during the interruptions) can be deleted now that this is done. (2026-07-20) - [ ] **4. Paper Mode redesign** — replace the `!important` recolor cascade with a scoped `data-theme="paper"` token approach; structural (not just color) changes to glass cards/code blocks/grain texture on reading routes.
## Backlog - [ ] **Run the `ai_graded` migration in Supabase:** `alter table public.quiz_sessions add column if not exists ai_graded boolean not null default false;` (in both `schema.sql` and `schema-quiz-tables-only.sql`, 2026-07-18) — required for the new `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT` cap to actually track usage; until run, the count query against a missing column will error and quiz `finish` will fall back to heuristic grading every time (safe fail-mode, but no AI grading at all until applied). - [ ] **Low-severity findings from the 2026-07-18 security audit, not yet fixed:** (1) a few API routes return raw Postgres error messages (`entryErr.message` etc.) directly in 500 responses — minor internal-detail leakage; (2) `gradeSession()` prompt (`src/lib/gemini.ts`) lacks the same "untrusted data" framing `extractKnowledge()` has, and its output isn't run through `sanitizeField()` before storage — self-scoped impact only (a user can only manipulate their own quiz score/feedback), but inconsistent with the rest of the codebase's pattern. - [ ] **Optional live-DB cleanup:** `profiles.xp` was dropped from `supabase/schema.sql` (2026-07-16, full XP removal) but NOT from the actual live Supabase database — no migration tooling/connection available to do it here. The column is harmless left in place (unused, always 0), but if you want it gone for real, run `ALTER TABLE public.profiles DROP COLUMN xp;` in the Supabase SQL editor. - [ ] **Run `supabase/schema-rag.sql` THEN `supabase/schema-rag-v2.sql` in Supabase** to activate RAG (v1: `vector` extension, `knowledge_chunks` + HNSW index; v2: `rag_documents` registry, `rag_index_pointers`, `rag_retrieval_logs`, hybrid search function). Both safe to re-run. Until then RAG is dormant (best-effort, no errors) and ingest/quiz behave exactly as before. **This item has been unchecked since the RAG layer was first added — confirm whether schema-rag.sql was actually ever run in production, since as far as these notes can tell it may still be dormant.** **Backfill:** existing entries/answers aren't embedded retroactively — either re-ingest, or write a one-off script that chunks+embeds existing `entries.raw_text` into `knowledge_chunks`. - [ ] **Cross-encoder reranking — evaluated, deliberately deferred (2026-07-15).** Three options considered: (a) local cross-encoder via `transformers.js`/ONNX (zero added Gemini quota, but a new ~25-90MB WASM dependency + cold-start latency on Vercel serverless), (b) Gemini LLM-based reranking (no new dependency, but breaks the "exactly 1 Gemini call per ingest" budget and adds latency/quota cost per retrieval), (c) hybrid-only, no reranker. **User chose (c).** Revisit if hybrid-only recall/precision proves insufficient at scale. - [ ] **Blog long-form from RAG:** blogs still compose from `summary`+`key_points` (zero-AI render). To generate richer articles grounded in retrieved chunks, add a `body` column populated at INGEST time (not per page view — keep renders AI-free), filled by a RAG-grounded Gemini call using `retrieveContext`. - [ ] **DB scaling (decided: stay on Supabase):** when 500MB is approached, upgrade to Supabase Pro ($25/mo, 8GB, no 7-day pause, backups) rather than migrating to TiDB. Watch vector storage — 768-dim HNSW is the main consumer; consider capping chunks/user or pruning old `source='answer'` chunks. TiDB was evaluated and rejected (no auth, no RLS — both load-bearing). - [ ] **Run schema-quiz-tables-only.sql in Supabase** (new tables: questions, facts, quiz_sessions, quiz_answers; now also adds `questions.correct_indices` for multi-select) — required before the new quiz flow works in real mode. If you get "policy already exists" errors, use this file instead of schema.sql; it safely handles policy conflicts. Already-created DBs just need the one-liner: `alter table public.questions add column if not exists correct_indices jsonb;` - [ ] Question-bank replenishment for old topics: topics ingested before the bank exists only get the synthesized fallback question; consider a one-off backfill (one Gemini call per legacy topic) or regenerate-on-empty. - [ ] Consider zero-shadow label variant if the soft glow still reads as a boundary. - [ ] Topic blogs currently compose from `summary` + `key_points` (no long-form field). If richer articles are wanted, add a `body`/`article` column populated at ingest by Gemini. - [ ] **Notes cross-device sync:** notes are localStorage-only (per-browser). To sync for signed-in users, add a Supabase `notes` table (id, user_id, parent_id, title, body, position, timestamps) + RLS, and branch `src/lib/notes.ts` demo(localStorage)/real(Supabase) like `data.ts`.
## Done (recent) - [x] **DSA: per-question diagrams + hellointerview-style rewrite for all 150 questions — COMPLETE 2026-07-21.** Every one of the 150 DSA question bodies (across all 18 `src/lib/dsa/topics/*.ts` chapter files) rewritten to a Signal / Brute-force / Optimal-approach / Diagram / Complexity / Thread structure, each with its own `viz:*` diagram (was 44/150 before, now 150/150). Done sequentially, chapter by chapter (not parallel agents, per explicit user request — this content is the site's main selling point). Also normalized the four chapters still on the older ASCII-art/Python-fence template (`two-pointers.ts`, `binary-search.ts`, `dp-1d.ts`, `dp-2d.ts`) to the prose-only house style in the same pass. Mid-rollout, a separate concurrent process added hardcoded `questions` (quiz) arrays to already-rewritten chapters — those were detected and preserved untouched throughout (verified via `grep -c 'kind:'` before/after each affected chapter). Verified clean via `npx tsx scripts/validate-viz.mts` (all 150 diagrams parse), `npx tsc --noEmit`, and a full `npm run build` (all 150 question pages + 18 chapter pages render as static HTML with no errors). Full per-question tracking in [DSA_HELLOINTERVIEW_CHECKLIST.md](DSA_HELLOINTERVIEW_CHECKLIST.md) (all boxes now checked). - [x] **DSA Pattern Atlas — complete** — static blog series covering the whole NeetCode 150 roadmap: 18 chapter blogs + 150 question blogs with one linear prev/next reading path. Content in `src/lib/dsa/topics/*.ts`, registry+nav helpers in `src/lib/dsa/index.ts`, routes `/blogs/dsa[/topic[/slug]]` (all SSG). Question list/difficulties verified against live neetcode.io (28E/101M/21H exact match); prereq/unlock DAG validated. Zero AI, zero DB. (2026-07-19, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Security audit + fixes: SSRF DNS-rebinding, quiz Gemini-quota abuse, curl-hardening** — full server-side audit (API auth, RLS, secrets, deps) with findings ranked by severity; fixed the two most serious: (1) SSRF via DNS rebinding in link ingestion, closed with a DNS-pinning `undici` dispatcher (`isBlockedIp`/`ssrfSafeDispatcher` in `src/app/api/ingest/route.ts`) verified against real `*.nip.io` rebinding domains; (2) unbounded Gemini-quota abuse via quiz sessions, fixed with a new independent `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT`=1/day cap (ingest's own cap lowered 20→9), plus a new "come back at X" popup on `/add` when the ingest cap is hit (timezone-correct now, was using server-local time). Also tightened `PUBLIC_PATHS` middleware matching from prefix to exact/segment match. (2026-07-18, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **XP feature removed entirely** — `xpForReview()` deleted, `awardXp()` renamed to `advanceStreak()` (streak logic only, no xp param), `xp` field dropped from `Profile`/`ReportCard`/`IngestResult` types and every API response/demo report builder, the visible `+{xp} XP` line removed from `/add`, `profiles.xp` column dropped from `supabase/schema.sql` (fresh-install reference only — see backlog for the live-DB column). (2026-07-16, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Ingest guardrails** — gibberish/spam rejection before the Gemini call (`src/lib/guardrails.ts`, dependency-free heuristics, tested against a 10-case suite); every field Gemini's extraction returns is now validated/clamped before DB insert (category allowlist, length caps); prompt-injection hardening in the extraction prompt (untrusted-data framing, relevant since link ingest feeds third-party page text into it); fixed a real `javascript:`/`data:` URI XSS gap in `Markdown.tsx`'s link renderer (verified live). **Same-day follow-up fix:** the guardrail was rejecting real articles (ASCII-divider false positive + a non-scale-invariant distinct-char check that broke on any prose past ~2 sentences) — both fixed, see SESSION_LOG. (2026-07-16, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **RAG pipeline hardening (v2)** — document registry with content-hash dedup (fixes duplicate-ingest bug), alias-based per-user index versioning + self-serve `/api/rag/reindex`, hybrid vector+BM25(tsvector) search with RRF fusion, and an observability layer (chunk-level retrieval logs, quality metrics, index-version/quiz-score correlation). Reranking evaluated and deferred. Requires `schema-rag-v2.sql`. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Rebrand: Engramia → Knovis** — new name (knowledge + vision, echoes "novice") across the whole app: Nav wordmark/tagline, Footer, login page, layout metadata, guided tour copy, profile page, About page origin story rewritten around the Knovis/novice theme, package.json, README. Guest cookie (`engramia_guest`→`knovis_guest`) and all localStorage/sessionStorage keys (tour, demo state, notes) renamed to a `knovis.*` prefix — old Engramia-era guest sessions/local data reset silently, no migration. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Tour interactivity + review-demo step + add-page demo gate** — spotlighted features (calendar, brain search/canvas) are now genuinely clickable during the tour (overlay `pointer-events:none` fix); link navigation blocked globally while touring so clicks can't route away; new self-contained "Try a review" mock-quiz step (14 steps total); `/add` shows an honest "This is a demo" popup instead of a fake simulated success. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Guided feature tour** — 13-step spotlight walkthrough of every feature (dashboard, brain, blogs, add) with translucent on-brand step cards; auto-runs on a browser's first visit (then hands off to /login) and replays on every "Continue as guest" click. `src/lib/tour.ts` + `src/components/TutorialTour.tsx` + `data-tour` anchors. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Attempted-tracking bug + security review + Gemini fallback + RAG + DB decision** — fixed local-day vs UTC done-tracking and the `?topic=` reroute (Suspense + `useSearchParams`); SSRF guard + daily ingest cap + JSON guards + auth-callback error redirect; Gemini model fallback chain; full RAG pipeline (chunk/embed/retrieve, `schema-rag.sql`, ingest grounding + answer indexing, best-effort); decided to stay on Supabase over TiDB. Demo progress now persists to localStorage. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Quiz-kind overhaul + day reports + blog Q&A + streak bar + footer/About** — flashcards fully removed; truefalse & multi-select question kinds added end-to-end; report card lists every question with full Q&A; calendar days clickable → day report modal; blog pages show their question bank with answers; streak-milestone progress bar on Momentum card; engram meaning tagline; global footer + quirky /about. (2026-07-14, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Rebranding, Mastery/XP Removal, True/False Statements & Review History Details** — Renamed Engram to Engramia globally, disabled XP/mastery metrics, replaced flashcards with graded True/False statement cards, show detailed prompt/answer/stored response breakdowns for already-done items, and change Start button to "See report" when daily plan is completed. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Review page bug fixes + /blogs page** — (1) done questions show "Already reviewed today" card instead of re-asking; (2) full-plan session now includes all items (done ones auto-skip) so report shows after all items not just 1; (3) stats "Due today" → "In plan" for consistency; (4) new `/blogs` library page + nav link, "Back to your brain" removed from blog pages. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Guest mode + plan polish** — fixed guest/demo loop on production (cookie + proxy), plan done-tracking (green dots, done-last, expandable), review error card instead of blank page, graph-card pills → blog pages. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **AI-call minimisation redesign** — ingest-time question bank (incl. MCQs) + facts, per-answer DB saves, single batch-grade report card, zero-AI plan narrative, fact of the day. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Install graphify CLI** — `pipx install graphifyy` (v0.9.14) at `/Users/bucc/.local/bin`; graph regenerated (384 nodes). (2026-07-13) - [x] **Personal notes + blog source** — `/notes` (localStorage, subnotes, markdown Write/Read), dashboard "Personal notes" section, blog source attribution (url vs "Text input"). (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Dashboard/brain polish** — "The thread today" rich insight, clickable plan rows → single-task review, topic blog pages (Fraunces serif), summarised brain card + Read-full, smaller 3D labels. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Deploy to Vercel** — migrated from Render. Removed `render.yaml`, connected Vercel CLI, deployed to production. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Fix Nav Auth UI** — fixed bug in `Nav.tsx` where the "Sign in" button was hidden for unauthenticated users when a database was connected. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] Plan markdown leak fix + progress calendar + /profile page + signup name (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] Brain label overlap fix + clickable labels + Space Grotesk (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG)

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
# Tasks
> One active milestone at a time. Move finished items to `SESSION_LOG.md`.
## Active milestone Redesign pass (4 parts, in order): 1. homepage story real estate ✅ → 2. rename Review→Recall ✅ → 3. content audit + visualizations (DSA/SQL/Macro) ✅ → 4. Paper Mode redesign. - [x] **1. Homepage story real estate** — flattened Today's plan back to one list (per-row series colour dot instead of per-story sections), capped `StoryJourney` to top 3 stories (due-today first, then most-recent) + dropped its chapter bead trail, added "All stories (N) →" link to `/blogs`; `/blogs`'s "Read as one story" module now floats started series to the top. (2026-07-20, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **2. Rename "Review" → "Recall"** — route `/review` → `/recall` (+ permanent redirect), nav pill, dashboard/footer/tour copy. Kept `reviews` DB table / SM-2 "review" terminology unchanged. (2026-07-20, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **3. Story content: accuracy pass + visualizations — COMPLETE.** All 34 chapter files (18 DSA, 12 SQL, 6 Macro incl. pilots) now have `viz:*` diagrams (91 total) and were read end-to-end for accuracy (8 real errors found and fixed). Competition Act stays paused from `/blogs` discovery. `npm run build` (331+ pages) + `tsc --noEmit` + the full `viz:*` payload validator all clean. Rolled out via 18 parallel background agents across 3 launch batches (2 interrupted by account session-limits mid-batch — each time verified zero corruption via `git status`/`tsc`/validator before resuming). Full per-file breakdown + accuracy-fix list in SESSION_LOG. `VIZ_ROLLOUT_CHECKLIST.md` (the resume-tracking doc used during the interruptions) can be deleted now that this is done. (2026-07-20) - [ ] **4. Paper Mode redesign** — replace the `!important` recolor cascade with a scoped `data-theme="paper"` token approach; structural (not just color) changes to glass cards/code blocks/grain texture on reading routes. **Partial (2026-07-21):** the transition animation was redone (wave → folding paper tiles) and several contrast leaks fixed (inline near-white rgba, viz SVG fills); the `!important`-cascade → token refactor itself is still open.
## Backlog - [ ] **Run the `ai_graded` migration in Supabase:** `alter table public.quiz_sessions add column if not exists ai_graded boolean not null default false;` (in both `schema.sql` and `schema-quiz-tables-only.sql`, 2026-07-18) — required for the new `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT` cap to actually track usage; until run, the count query against a missing column will error and quiz `finish` will fall back to heuristic grading every time (safe fail-mode, but no AI grading at all until applied). - [ ] **Low-severity findings from the 2026-07-18 security audit, not yet fixed:** (1) a few API routes return raw Postgres error messages (`entryErr.message` etc.) directly in 500 responses — minor internal-detail leakage; (2) `gradeSession()` prompt (`src/lib/gemini.ts`) lacks the same "untrusted data" framing `extractKnowledge()` has, and its output isn't run through `sanitizeField()` before storage — self-scoped impact only (a user can only manipulate their own quiz score/feedback), but inconsistent with the rest of the codebase's pattern. - [ ] **Optional live-DB cleanup:** `profiles.xp` was dropped from `supabase/schema.sql` (2026-07-16, full XP removal) but NOT from the actual live Supabase database — no migration tooling/connection available to do it here. The column is harmless left in place (unused, always 0), but if you want it gone for real, run `ALTER TABLE public.profiles DROP COLUMN xp;` in the Supabase SQL editor. - [ ] **Run `supabase/schema-rag.sql` THEN `supabase/schema-rag-v2.sql` in Supabase** to activate RAG (v1: `vector` extension, `knowledge_chunks` + HNSW index; v2: `rag_documents` registry, `rag_index_pointers`, `rag_retrieval_logs`, hybrid search function). Both safe to re-run. Until then RAG is dormant (best-effort, no errors) and ingest/quiz behave exactly as before. **This item has been unchecked since the RAG layer was first added — confirm whether schema-rag.sql was actually ever run in production, since as far as these notes can tell it may still be dormant.** **Backfill:** existing entries/answers aren't embedded retroactively — either re-ingest, or write a one-off script that chunks+embeds existing `entries.raw_text` into `knowledge_chunks`. - [ ] **Cross-encoder reranking — evaluated, deliberately deferred (2026-07-15).** Three options considered: (a) local cross-encoder via `transformers.js`/ONNX (zero added Gemini quota, but a new ~25-90MB WASM dependency + cold-start latency on Vercel serverless), (b) Gemini LLM-based reranking (no new dependency, but breaks the "exactly 1 Gemini call per ingest" budget and adds latency/quota cost per retrieval), (c) hybrid-only, no reranker. **User chose (c).** Revisit if hybrid-only recall/precision proves insufficient at scale. - [ ] **Blog long-form from RAG:** blogs still compose from `summary`+`key_points` (zero-AI render). To generate richer articles grounded in retrieved chunks, add a `body` column populated at INGEST time (not per page view — keep renders AI-free), filled by a RAG-grounded Gemini call using `retrieveContext`. - [ ] **DB scaling (decided: stay on Supabase):** when 500MB is approached, upgrade to Supabase Pro ($25/mo, 8GB, no 7-day pause, backups) rather than migrating to TiDB. Watch vector storage — 768-dim HNSW is the main consumer; consider capping chunks/user or pruning old `source='answer'` chunks. TiDB was evaluated and rejected (no auth, no RLS — both load-bearing). - [ ] **Run schema-quiz-tables-only.sql in Supabase** (new tables: questions, facts, quiz_sessions, quiz_answers; now also adds `questions.correct_indices` for multi-select) — required before the new quiz flow works in real mode. If you get "policy already exists" errors, use this file instead of schema.sql; it safely handles policy conflicts. Already-created DBs just need the one-liner: `alter table public.questions add column if not exists correct_indices jsonb;` - [ ] Question-bank replenishment for old topics: topics ingested before the bank exists only get the synthesized fallback question; consider a one-off backfill (one Gemini call per legacy topic) or regenerate-on-empty. - [ ] Consider zero-shadow label variant if the soft glow still reads as a boundary. - [ ] Topic blogs currently compose from `summary` + `key_points` (no long-form field). If richer articles are wanted, add a `body`/`article` column populated at ingest by Gemini. - [ ] **Notes cross-device sync:** notes are localStorage-only (per-browser). To sync for signed-in users, add a Supabase `notes` table (id, user_id, parent_id, title, body, position, timestamps) + RLS, and branch `src/lib/notes.ts` demo(localStorage)/real(Supabase) like `data.ts`.
## Done (recent) - [x] **Blog/recall/paper-mode fixes (2026-07-21):** inline LaTeX-ish math rendering in `Markdown.tsx` (`$O(1)$`/`$\text{…}$`/sup-sub/greek, with a currency guard so `$1 billion` stays currency); per-series topic-wise report cards in `/recall` (each series group = its own session + inline report on submit; demo sessions now a Map keyed by id); collapsible "Learn from our experts" dropdown on `/blogs` + a "Want a dedicated story?" write-to-us modal (mailto); paper-mode transition changed from a reveal wave to folding paper tiles; paper-mode near-white contrast leaks fixed (inline rgba titles + viz SVG fills). See SESSION_LOG. - [x] **DSA: per-question diagrams + hellointerview-style rewrite for all 150 questions — COMPLETE 2026-07-21.** Every one of the 150 DSA question bodies (across all 18 `src/lib/dsa/topics/*.ts` chapter files) rewritten to a Signal / Brute-force / Optimal-approach / Diagram / Complexity / Thread structure, each with its own `viz:*` diagram (was 44/150 before, now 150/150). Done sequentially, chapter by chapter (not parallel agents, per explicit user request — this content is the site's main selling point). Also normalized the four chapters still on the older ASCII-art/Python-fence template (`two-pointers.ts`, `binary-search.ts`, `dp-1d.ts`, `dp-2d.ts`) to the prose-only house style in the same pass. Mid-rollout, a separate concurrent process added hardcoded `questions` (quiz) arrays to already-rewritten chapters — those were detected and preserved untouched throughout (verified via `grep -c 'kind:'` before/after each affected chapter). Verified clean via `npx tsx scripts/validate-viz.mts` (all 150 diagrams parse), `npx tsc --noEmit`, and a full `npm run build` (all 150 question pages + 18 chapter pages render as static HTML with no errors). Full per-question tracking in [DSA_HELLOINTERVIEW_CHECKLIST.md](DSA_HELLOINTERVIEW_CHECKLIST.md) (all boxes now checked). - [x] **DSA Pattern Atlas — complete** — static blog series covering the whole NeetCode 150 roadmap: 18 chapter blogs + 150 question blogs with one linear prev/next reading path. Content in `src/lib/dsa/topics/*.ts`, registry+nav helpers in `src/lib/dsa/index.ts`, routes `/blogs/dsa[/topic[/slug]]` (all SSG). Question list/difficulties verified against live neetcode.io (28E/101M/21H exact match); prereq/unlock DAG validated. Zero AI, zero DB. (2026-07-19, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Security audit + fixes: SSRF DNS-rebinding, quiz Gemini-quota abuse, curl-hardening** — full server-side audit (API auth, RLS, secrets, deps) with findings ranked by severity; fixed the two most serious: (1) SSRF via DNS rebinding in link ingestion, closed with a DNS-pinning `undici` dispatcher (`isBlockedIp`/`ssrfSafeDispatcher` in `src/app/api/ingest/route.ts`) verified against real `*.nip.io` rebinding domains; (2) unbounded Gemini-quota abuse via quiz sessions, fixed with a new independent `QUIZ_AI_DAILY_LIMIT`=1/day cap (ingest's own cap lowered 20→9), plus a new "come back at X" popup on `/add` when the ingest cap is hit (timezone-correct now, was using server-local time). Also tightened `PUBLIC_PATHS` middleware matching from prefix to exact/segment match. (2026-07-18, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **XP feature removed entirely** — `xpForReview()` deleted, `awardXp()` renamed to `advanceStreak()` (streak logic only, no xp param), `xp` field dropped from `Profile`/`ReportCard`/`IngestResult` types and every API response/demo report builder, the visible `+{xp} XP` line removed from `/add`, `profiles.xp` column dropped from `supabase/schema.sql` (fresh-install reference only — see backlog for the live-DB column). (2026-07-16, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Ingest guardrails** — gibberish/spam rejection before the Gemini call (`src/lib/guardrails.ts`, dependency-free heuristics, tested against a 10-case suite); every field Gemini's extraction returns is now validated/clamped before DB insert (category allowlist, length caps); prompt-injection hardening in the extraction prompt (untrusted-data framing, relevant since link ingest feeds third-party page text into it); fixed a real `javascript:`/`data:` URI XSS gap in `Markdown.tsx`'s link renderer (verified live). **Same-day follow-up fix:** the guardrail was rejecting real articles (ASCII-divider false positive + a non-scale-invariant distinct-char check that broke on any prose past ~2 sentences) — both fixed, see SESSION_LOG. (2026-07-16, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **RAG pipeline hardening (v2)** — document registry with content-hash dedup (fixes duplicate-ingest bug), alias-based per-user index versioning + self-serve `/api/rag/reindex`, hybrid vector+BM25(tsvector) search with RRF fusion, and an observability layer (chunk-level retrieval logs, quality metrics, index-version/quiz-score correlation). Reranking evaluated and deferred. Requires `schema-rag-v2.sql`. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Rebrand: Engramia → Knovis** — new name (knowledge + vision, echoes "novice") across the whole app: Nav wordmark/tagline, Footer, login page, layout metadata, guided tour copy, profile page, About page origin story rewritten around the Knovis/novice theme, package.json, README. Guest cookie (`engramia_guest`→`knovis_guest`) and all localStorage/sessionStorage keys (tour, demo state, notes) renamed to a `knovis.*` prefix — old Engramia-era guest sessions/local data reset silently, no migration. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Tour interactivity + review-demo step + add-page demo gate** — spotlighted features (calendar, brain search/canvas) are now genuinely clickable during the tour (overlay `pointer-events:none` fix); link navigation blocked globally while touring so clicks can't route away; new self-contained "Try a review" mock-quiz step (14 steps total); `/add` shows an honest "This is a demo" popup instead of a fake simulated success. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Guided feature tour** — 13-step spotlight walkthrough of every feature (dashboard, brain, blogs, add) with translucent on-brand step cards; auto-runs on a browser's first visit (then hands off to /login) and replays on every "Continue as guest" click. `src/lib/tour.ts` + `src/components/TutorialTour.tsx` + `data-tour` anchors. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Attempted-tracking bug + security review + Gemini fallback + RAG + DB decision** — fixed local-day vs UTC done-tracking and the `?topic=` reroute (Suspense + `useSearchParams`); SSRF guard + daily ingest cap + JSON guards + auth-callback error redirect; Gemini model fallback chain; full RAG pipeline (chunk/embed/retrieve, `schema-rag.sql`, ingest grounding + answer indexing, best-effort); decided to stay on Supabase over TiDB. Demo progress now persists to localStorage. (2026-07-15, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Quiz-kind overhaul + day reports + blog Q&A + streak bar + footer/About** — flashcards fully removed; truefalse & multi-select question kinds added end-to-end; report card lists every question with full Q&A; calendar days clickable → day report modal; blog pages show their question bank with answers; streak-milestone progress bar on Momentum card; engram meaning tagline; global footer + quirky /about. (2026-07-14, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Rebranding, Mastery/XP Removal, True/False Statements & Review History Details** — Renamed Engram to Engramia globally, disabled XP/mastery metrics, replaced flashcards with graded True/False statement cards, show detailed prompt/answer/stored response breakdowns for already-done items, and change Start button to "See report" when daily plan is completed. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Review page bug fixes + /blogs page** — (1) done questions show "Already reviewed today" card instead of re-asking; (2) full-plan session now includes all items (done ones auto-skip) so report shows after all items not just 1; (3) stats "Due today" → "In plan" for consistency; (4) new `/blogs` library page + nav link, "Back to your brain" removed from blog pages. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Guest mode + plan polish** — fixed guest/demo loop on production (cookie + proxy), plan done-tracking (green dots, done-last, expandable), review error card instead of blank page, graph-card pills → blog pages. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **AI-call minimisation redesign** — ingest-time question bank (incl. MCQs) + facts, per-answer DB saves, single batch-grade report card, zero-AI plan narrative, fact of the day. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Install graphify CLI** — `pipx install graphifyy` (v0.9.14) at `/Users/bucc/.local/bin`; graph regenerated (384 nodes). (2026-07-13) - [x] **Personal notes + blog source** — `/notes` (localStorage, subnotes, markdown Write/Read), dashboard "Personal notes" section, blog source attribution (url vs "Text input"). (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Dashboard/brain polish** — "The thread today" rich insight, clickable plan rows → single-task review, topic blog pages (Fraunces serif), summarised brain card + Read-full, smaller 3D labels. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Deploy to Vercel** — migrated from Render. Removed `render.yaml`, connected Vercel CLI, deployed to production. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] **Fix Nav Auth UI** — fixed bug in `Nav.tsx` where the "Sign in" button was hidden for unauthenticated users when a database was connected. (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] Plan markdown leak fix + progress calendar + /profile page + signup name (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG) - [x] Brain label overlap fix + clickable labels + Space Grotesk (2026-07-13, see SESSION_LOG)
